CHAPTER 554 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to amend and reduce into one
the severalactsin relationto the road law of Greenup county," approved
January 24th, 1871.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled" An acttoamend and reduce
into one the several acts in relation to the road law of
Greenup county," approved January 24th,1871,be,and
the same is hereby, amended , that in all cases where
roads in said county have been made and established
according to law and the acts to which thisis an amend-
ment ,and personsor owners of land on eithersidethere-
of have set their fence or fences out in said road as thus
established, and thereby obstructed the travel thereon
and use thereof, the overseeror surveyor of said road
shallhave the right to remove said fence therefrom, or
cause the same to be removed , aftergiving to the per-
son or owner as aforesaid ten days' notice thereof, and
shall charge the expense of said removal to such person
or owner ; and if not paid on presentation,the said over-
seer or surveyor may recover the same ,together with the
costsof action, from such person or owner , before the
county judge of the county,or any justiceof the peace
thereof: Provided, Such person or owner does not,before
the expirationof said notice, remove the fencehimself.
§2 . That when a fence shallbe erected in or across
Penaltyfora publicroad of thecounty,the personerecting,or caus-
publicing to be erected,such fence,shallpay a fineof one dol-
larforevery twenty-four hours the fence shallcontinue
in or across the road . If the owner or occupant of the

land upon which said fence is erected shall,afternotice
from the overseer or surveyor of the road ,failto remove
said fence,he shallbe fined one dollarfor every twenty-
four hours such fence may remain in or across said road
after such notice; and it shall be the duty of the sur-
veyor or overseer to give said notice.
Penaltyforfell-
ing trees across§3 . That any person who shallfella treeinto any

withintwenty-fourhours,or shallcut,pullup,or destroy

forevery such offense; and any person who shall will-

to a fine not exceeding fiftydollars; the said penalty in
this and the foregoing section may be sued for and
recovered,by action in the name of the Commonwealth ,
beforeany justiceof the peace,or the county judge of
said county ; and , after paying the expense of recovery,
the remainder to go to the improvement of the road in
the districtwhere the offense was committed or obstruc-

§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .

CHAPTER 579 .
AN ACT for the benefitof JeffersonRitchie,a pauper idiot of Nicholas
county .
WHEREAS ,It appearsears that JeffersonRitchie,a pauper
idiot of Nicholas county ,from an oversight by his com-
mittee in failingto have a re-inquest held within the time
requiredby law,did not draw his allowance for the six
months ending March , 1876; and whereas, it appears
that said idiotis stillliving, and is a pauper ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditorof PublicAccounts be,and he is
hereby ,directed to draw his warrant upon the Treasury ,
payable out of any money not otherwiseappropriated,to
Cassandra Tinchee , committee for said Jefferson Ritchie ,
a pauper idiotof Nicholascounty,forthe sum of thirty-
seven dollarsand fiftycents for the benefitof said idiot.
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 20, 1878.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 37
CHAPTER 580 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof David Woods , of Crittendencounty .
WHEREAS , Nancy Rebecca Susan Gordon Edwards , of
Crittenden county , was ,by the Crittenden circuitcourt,
duly adjudged to be an idiot; and whereas ,David Woods ,
of said county , was by said court duly appointed a com-
mittee of said Edwards ; and whereas ,the said court,at
itsJune term , 1876, made an order showing that it ap-
peared to the satisfaction of the court that said idiot was
then stillalive, and in the care of her said committee ,
and thatshe had not sufficientestateforher support,and
that her mother was stillunable to support her,and al-
lowing the said David Woods , as such committee , the
sum of thirty-seven dollarsand fiftycents for the support
of said idiotfrom the 13th day of December , 1875 , until
the 13thday of June,1876,and orderingsaidorderto be
copied and certifiedto the Auditor for payment ; and
whereas , more than five years elapsed after the said
Edwards was so adjudged an idiot, and before the said
order was made , at the June term of said court,in the
year 1876 ; and whereas ,on that account the saidAuditor
has not paid the said sum of thirty-seven dollarsandfifty
cents,or any part thereof; and whereas ,the said David
Woods necessarilyand in good faithexpended the said
sum of thirty-seven dollarsand fiftycents for the support
of the said idiotfrom the 13th day of December , 1875,to
the said 13th day of June , 1876 ; therefore,
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said David Woods be , and he is hereby ,
allowed the sum of thirty-seven dollars and fiftycents,
payable out of the Public Treasury ,for and on account
of money expended by him as aforesaid, and that the
Auditor of Public Accounts draw his warrant on the
public Treasurer in favor of the said David Woods for
that amount .
§ 2. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved March 20, 1878.

CHAPTER 581 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Joseph P. Ringo ,of Carrollcounty .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Joseph P. Ringo , for his services as elisor
for the county of Carroll during a special term of the
criminal court of said county ,beginning on the day of
November , 1877, and continuing nine judicial days , for
the trialof the sheriffand deputy sheriffof said county ,
and four others,charged with the crime of murder ,be,
and is hereby , allowed the sum of twenty -five dollars,
and that the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and ishereby ,
directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of
the said Joseph P. Ringo for the same .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
-Approved March 20, 1878.

CHAPTER 583 .
AN ACT declaringCow creek, in Estillcounty,a navigablestream.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Cow creek,in Estillcounty ,be ,and the same
ishereby ,declared a navigable stream from its mouth to
W. T. Barns ' house .
§2 . The land -owners on said creek are allowed to
keep up theirwater -gaps : Provided, Said gaps will not
hinder the navigation of said stream at any or alltimes.
§3 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 20 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 584 .
AN ACT for the benefitof ElizabethCollins, of Elliottcounty .
WHEREAS ,It appears that the State,in 1869, purchased
a tractof land belonging to Squire H. Hunter ,in Elliott
county, Kentucky ; and that in 1872 Elizabeth Collins
purchased about 105 acresof saidland- the whole tract
embraced 840 acres; and whereas , it appears that the
Statedid not procure a deed to saidland until1875,and
that the purchase made by said Collinswas made in good
faithand without notice of the purchase by the State,
and that the residue of said land is worth the amount bid
by the State; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the State hereby releasesallitstitlein and to
the said 105 acres purchased by said Collinsfrom Squire
H. Hunter unto the said Elizabeth Collins, her heirs or
assigns,forever;and foreverrelinquishitsrightor claim
to said land under the purchase aforesaid.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 20, 1878.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 43
CHAPTER 585 .
AN ACT to authorizethe erectionof a toll-gatenear the Great Crossings, on
the Great Crossingsand Stamping Ground Turnpike Road ,inScottcounty.
WHEREAS ,It isrepresentedto the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky that unjust advantages
are being taken of the Great Crossings and Stamping
Ground Turnpike Road ,in Scott county ,by persons avoid-
ing the gate where itnow stands,by passing around said
gate through farms and otherprivatepassways,and by
persons livingabove the gate and using the road ,cutting
itto pieces with heavy loaded wagons and other travel,
therebygettingthe benefitof the road and paying noth-
ing to keep itin repair; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the managers of the
road to erect a toll-gate for the collection of tolls near
the Great Crossings on said road : Provided, The rate of
tollscollected at said gate shallbe in proportion to the
distance traveled and in accordance with the rates allowed
by law for each and every five miles traveled.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved March 20, 1878 .
CHAPTER 586 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county clerk of Cumberland county to procure
suitable books and indexes for the purpose of transcribing the mutilated
survey recordsin the surveyor'sofficeof saidcounty, appointing C. L. S.
Matthews to transcribesaid records,and making itthe duty of the county
judgeof saidcountytopay him therefor,assoonasthework iscompleted,
at the ratesallowed by law to circuitclerksforcopying deeds,plats,& c .
WHEREAS ,It is a fact that all the records of warrant
and other surveys of Cumberland county ,Kentucky ,con-
tained in books A ,B ,C ,and D ,together with the indexes
thereto belonging , were never made in suitable books ,
and such as they were ,they have , by age and constant
use, become so dirty and mutilated that many of the
recordsare almostillegible,and consequentlyunsafeand
comparatively worthless as public records; and whereas ,

CHAPTER 597 .
AN ACT to definethe boundary line between the countiesof Floyd and
Johnson .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundary line between the counties of
Floyd and Johnson shallbe as hereafterdefined: Begin-
ing at the Magoffin county line, at the head of Jennie's
creek; thence with the dividing ridge,between Jennie's
creek and Middle creek , to the head of Abbot creek ;
thence with the dividing ridge, between Jennie's creek
and Abbot creek,to the head of the Lick Fork of Jennie's
creek; thence with the dividing ridge,between the Lick
Fork of Jennie'screekand Millcreek,to theFloyd coun-
ty line, where itis now located at the head of the Stone
Coal Hollow .
§2 . That hereafterthe boundary linebetween the coun-
tiesof Floyd and Johnson shallbe as defined in the first
section of this act, so far as it goes .
§3 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 20, 1878.

CHAPTER 613 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Levi Wilcher , of Casey county.
WHEREAS , The Governor of thisCommonwealth did,on
the 28th day of June,1876,offera reward of two hund-
red dollars for the arrest and delivery to the jailer of
Casey county, and convictionof one Thomas Moore ,
charged with the offenseof malicious shooting and wound-
ing; and whereas,on the 10th day of March ,1877,Levi
Wilcher did arrest said Thomas Moore ,and deliver him to
the jailerof said county ; and whereas ,the said Thomas
Moore was killed before a trial and conviction was ob-
tained; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and is
hereby , authorized and directed to draw his warrant on
the Treasurer of this Commonwealth in favor of Levi
Wilcher,forthe sum of two hundred dollars,to be paid
out of any money in the Treasury not otherwiseappro-
priated.
§2 . That this act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved March 21, 1878.

CHAPTER 626 .
AN ACT for the benefit of A. L. Coldiron ,late sheriffof Harlan county .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That A. L. Coldiron,latesheriffof Harlan county,
be ,and he is,allowed the furthertime of two years, from
and afterthe passage of thisact,to collecthis unpaid
fee-billsdue him in said county for the years 1875 and
1876 .

fee-bills as other sheriffs of this Commonwealth shall

forthe collectionof illegaltaxes and fee-bills.

Approved March 22, 1878.

CHAPTER 627 .
AN ACT to amend an act authorizingthe Ballard county courtto levy an
ad valorem tax .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the money raisedunder the provisionsof
sectionsone and four of said act be appliedto the in-
debtedness of said county as the county court may ,by
order entered of record,direct,a majority of the justices
of the peace composing the court concurring therein.
§2 . That the tax leviedfor the year 1877 is hereby
legalized and made as binding as the tax levied under
said act for the year 1876 .
§3 . That the provisions of said act relating to an ad
valorem tax, shall continue in force, as hereby directed,
forthree years from the firstday of January , 1878, and
no longer .
§ 4. All laws in conflictwith this act are hereby re-
pealed .
§5 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 22 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 628 .
AN ACT to regulatethe duties of the sheriffof Pendleton county in cer-
tain causes .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafteritshallbe the duty of the sheriffof
Pendleton county, on or before receivingthe tax-book
from the county clerkeach year,to providehimself with
a well-bound blank book, properlyruled, in which he
shall,withinten days aftercollectingany tax on histax-
book forsuch year,or certifiedtohim as providedin sec-
tionstwenty-threeand twenty-four,articleeight,chapter
ninety-two, or sectionsix, articletwo, chaptertwenty-
seven , of the General Statutes, make an entry of the
same in said book,which entryshallshow thefullname
of thetax-payer,thenature,amount,and dateof collec-

tion of each item of tax so collected, and whether the
same was collectedby sale, attachment, or otherwise,
which book shallbe accessible,at alltimes,forinspection
by any tax-payer ,his agent or attorney,or other person
interested therein .
§2 . The names of the tax-payers in such voting pre-
cinct in saidcounty shallbe entered in said book together,
but separate from the names of tax-payers in other pre-
cincts .
§3 . Said sheriffshall, on making any settlement with

quire it, produce said book to said court for examina-

present to the county court the Auditor'squietusfor the

to said court,and thereafteritshallremain in the custody

§5 . After said book is in the custody of the clerk,as
contemplated in the preceding section, any sheriff, or
other collectingofficer, who shall collectany tax mem-
tioned in the firstsection,and due in the year to which
said book relates, shall make an entry thereof in said
book,as thesheriffisrequiredto do by the firstsection
of thisact,and the county clerk shallattestsuch entry.
§ 6. If the sheriffor other officercollectingany tax,as
contemplatedherein,shallfail,forthe space of ten days,
to enterthe same in saidbook,as requiredby thisact,he
shall be fined for each offense not less than ten nor more
than twenty dollars; and if any such sheriffor officer
shall,knowingly and corruptly,make a falseentryin said
book , he shall be fined for each offense not less than
twenty-fivenor more than one hundred dollars, to be
recoveredby the indictmentof a grand jury.

passage .
Approved March 22, 1878.

CHAPTER 629 .
AN ACT toprevent stock from running at large in Shelby county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of directorsof any turnpikeroad,
or the overseer of any dirt road,in Shelby county ,hav-
ing theirroad or roads continuallyfenced for two miles
or more ,whenever they are petitionedby a majorityof
the land -owners along the lineof theirroad ,shall have
power to prevent stock of any kind from running at large
upon the road under their control: Provided, That writ-
ten or printed noticesshall have been posted along the
lineof such road or roads for thirtydays,settingforth
that no stock is allowed to run at large upon such roads.
After such noticesshallhave been posted, any person
permittinghisor her stockto run at largeon such roads
shall be fined not less than five nor more than ten dollars,
which fine shall be recovered before a justice of the
peace of the districtin which the offense is committed ,
and the stock so offending shall be liable for whatever
finemay be imposed .
§2 . The board of directors, or the overseer of any
road, may , in theirdiscretion, grant permits allowing
stockto run atlargeupon theirroad,under such restric-
tionsas they may think proper .
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved March 22, 1878 .

CHAPTER 632 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Richmond and Lancaster Turnpike
Road Company .
WHEREAS , Under the second section of an act,entitled
"An act to chartersundry turnpikeroad companies in
Madison county ," approved February 26 , 1849, a com-
pany was organized, and sufficientstock taken, and
money paid in, to constructa macadamized road from
the town of Richmond to Lancaster, that part of the
roadsituatedinMadisoncountybeingunder thecontrol
S

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 117
and management of the president and board of directors
residentwithin Madison county, and the extent of this
branch of said road being from Richmond to Paint Lick
creek; and whereas , in the judgment of the board of
directors and the stockholders,certain changes should be
made in the route and bed of said road,and in the grade
of said road, at certainpointson the lineof same ,and
other improvements in bridges, causeways,and culverts
should be made ; and doubts have been expressed as to
the power and authorityof said board of directorsto
cause such changes in the routeofthisroad and improve-
ments aforesaid ; now ,therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That power and authorityare hereby conferred
upon the president and board of directorsof the Rich-

CHAPTER 633 .
AN ACT to repeal an act, entitled" An act to authorizethe judge of the
Jeffersoncounty court to appoint a collectorof back taxes for Jefferson
county," which went into effectFebruary 24th, 1873 , and to direct the
Auditor to reconvey to partiestheirproperty which has been sold under
the provisions of said act.
WHEREAS , An act was passed by the Legislature of Ken-
tucky, entitled" An act to authorizethe judge of the
Jefferson county court to appoint a collector of back
taxes for Jefferson county ," which went into effect24th
February , 1873, and sales were made of many lots and
parcelsof land in said county to compel the payment of
delinquent taxes therein, whilst no such sales were au-
thorized in the other counties of this State ; and the said
sales,conveyances,and proceedingsviolatedthe princi-
plesof equalityand uniformity,which shouldbe observed
in collectingthe general revenue of the Commonwealth ;
now , therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That in all cases where sales have been made
under authorityof said act of 24th February, 1873, to
collectback taxes in Jeffersoncounty , or when the Aud-
itorhas executedconveyances under theauthorityof said
act,conveyingthe privatepropertyof the owners of lots
and lands in Jefferson county , or by any other act or
saleorconveyance,which would be ineffectualtotrans-
fer,bind,or convey lands to satisfydelinquentor back.
taxesin any othercounty of thisState,shallbe nulland
void .
§2 . That the act of the 24th February, 1873,entitled
" An act to authorize the judge of the Jefferson county
courtto appointa collectorof back taxes," ishereby re-
pealed and declared null and void.
§3 . Nothing in this act shallbe so construed as to re-
leasethe lienwhich the Commonwealth may have by the
general law regulating revenue and taxation; but the
Auditor,upon the requestof owners whose propertyhas
been conveyed to the Commonwealth of Kentucky by
him ,shallexecutea properreleaseto such owner,relin-

§1 . All officersand agentsemployed by,or by author-

or malt liquorsbehind screens,frostedwindows ,or any

taurants,drug stores, groceries, coffee-houses , cellars,or
other places of public resort,wherein intoxicating, spirit-
ous, or vinous liquorsare soldin violationof thisact,
shallbe shut up and abated as public nuisances,upon the
conviction of the keeper thereof, and shallbe punished
as herein provided .

liquors,with or without license,cause the intoxicationof
any other person at his place of sellingliquorswithin the

ployer,or otherperson who shallbe injuredin person or
property,or means of support,by any intoxicatedperson,
or in consequence of the intoxication,habitualor other-
wise,of any person,shallhave a rightof actionin hisor
her name ,severally or jointly, against any person who
shall,by sellingor giving intoxicatingliquors,have caused
the intoxication,in whole or in part,of such person or
persons,and in which actionexemplary damages may be
awarded ; and married women shallhave the same right

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 161
to bring suits,and to control the same ,and the amount
recovered, as a feme sole; and alldamages recovered by
a minor under thisact shallbe paid eitherto such minor
or his or her parent ,guardian , or next friend, as the court
shalldirect; and the unlawful saleor giving away of intox-
icatingliquorsshallwork a forfeitureof allrightsof the
lesseeor tenantunder any leaseor contractof rentupon
the premises where such unlawful sale or giving away

chartermay be by appropriateactioninthe mayor'scourt
of the cityof Ashland ; and in such cases the jurisdiction

fineor costsdue said city.

of Kentucky :

ler, W. W. Orr , C. R. McCormick ,N. J. Strouler, James
A. Powers ,J. H. Holton ,T. M. Dorce ,C. S Savage ,J. R.
Wilson ,B. F. Taylor , W. A. Taliaferro, and R. M. Byar ,
of Bracken county , and Evan Lloyd , W. T. Sallie, S. T.
Pollock,John Mannen ,E. C. Dimmitt,J. Henry Walton ,
Jas .Mitchell, L. S. Lutteral, S. H. Clift, William Clark,
of Mason county, be, and are hereby, appointed com-
missioners, under the direction of any three of whom
subscriptionsmay be receivedtothe capitalstockof the
Augusta ,Germantown and Johnson's Junction Railroad
Company , which is hereby incorporated; and they may
open books as the above named commissioners , or any
three of them ,may direct,to receive subscriptionsto the
capitalstockof said company ; and if any of said com-

as such .
§2 . That the capital stock of said Augusta ,German-
town and Johnson's Junction Railroad Company shallbe

increased to fivehundred thousand dollarsby the board
of directorshereinafterprovidedfor,which may be sub-
scribed by individuals or a corporation, and divided

installments as the directorsmay order; and as soon as
one hundred and fiftyshares of said stock shallbe sub-
scribed,thesubscribers,theirsuccessorsand assigns,shall
be, and they are hereby declared, incorporatedinto a
company ,by the name and styleof the " Augusta,Ger-
mantown and Johnson'sJunctionRailroadCompany ;"
and by thatname shallbe capableof purchasing,hold-
ing, selling, leasing, and conveying real and personal
estate,sofaras thesame may be necessaryforthe pur-
poses of the corporation,and no more ; and shallhave

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 165
perpetualsuccession, and by said corporatename may
sue and be sued , contract and be contracted with ; and
may have and use a common seal,and alter and renew
the same at pleasure ; and shallhave all the rightsand
privilegeswhich other corporate bodies may lawfully do .
§3 . That it shallbe the duty of the commissioners ,or
those who may act as such,to give notice in such manner
as they may think best,ofthe time and place of opening
books to receive subscriptionsto the capitalstock of said
company . And if,at the expirationof the time forwhich

cent.of the amount of subscriptionpaid in,said commis -

tionbooks before the subscribersthen present ;and there-
upon ,said subscribers,or a majority of them then present,
shallhave power to elect,by ballot,a president,secretary,
and seven directors; and said directorsmay allow such
compensation for the servicesof the president and secre-
taryas they may think proper, the secretaryto act as
treasurer of the company , having executed a bond to
secure said company against loss. At such election,and
all other elections, each stockholder shall be allowed one
vote for each and every share of stock owned by such

other person to act for them as proxy for it,him , or her;
and said commissioners ,or any three of them ,shallhold
and be the judges of the firstelection; and to continue
the succession of the president and directorsof said com-
pany , a president,secretary, and seven directorsshallbe
chosen annuallyon the firstMonday in June next suc-
ceeding after the organization of the company ; said
annual election to be conducted by the board , or such
persons as they may designateto hold saidelections. If
a vacancy should occur in said board from any cause
beforethe end of theyear forwhich theywere chosen,
1

166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the board of directorsshallhave power to fillthe vacancy
forthe unexpiredportionof the year: Provided,No one

by the presidentand directors,or a majorityof the di-
rectors, at any time to fillsuch vacancy or the transac-

§4 . That the company organized in pursuance of the
foregoingsectionsof thisact are hereby vested with all
powers,privileges,and immunitieswhich are,or may be,
necessary to construct,complete , and operate a railroad,
with double or singletrack,from a point within the corpo-

near thereto, to a point via Germantown ,in Bracken and
Mason counties, at or near Johnson's Station, on the
Kentucky Central Railroad,Northern Division, in Flem-
ing county ,Kentucky ; and forthat purpose to enter upon
and take possessionof so much land , and make any nec-
essarysurveys as may be necessaryto construct, com-
plete,and operatesaidrailroadand itsappendages; and
if said company cannot agree with the owners of said
land taken as aforesaid,applicationmay be made to any
justiceof the peace in the county wherein said land may
be situated,who ,thereupon, shallissuehis warrant,di-
rectedto the sheriffof his county,to summon a jury of
twentybonafidehousekeepers,not relatedor otherwisein-
terested,to meet on the land, or as near it as practica-
ble,to be valued , on a day named in said warrant ,not
lessthan ten nor more than twenty days afterthe issuing
the same ; and ifany of the jurorsfailto attend, said
sheriffshallforthwithsummon as many jurorsas may be
necessary, with those in attendance, to complete their
pannel aforesaid; andfrom them each party,orthe attor-
ney or agent of eitherparty,ifpresent,ifnot,then the
sherifffor the party absent, may strike off four jurors
each, and the remaining twelve shall act as a jury of
inquestof damages . After having been sworn by the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 167
sheriffjustly and impartiallyto fix the damages which
the owner or owners of the land shall sustain by reason
of the use and occupation of the same by said company ,
the summons issuedby the justice of the peace shall be
served on the owner or occupant of the land proposed to
be taken ,or upon the agent or attorney, and if infants,
then upon the guardian,at leastten days beforesaidin-
quest. In estimating the damages , the jury shall take
into consideration the benefits resulting to the owner
from conducting saidroad though the lands of said owner ,
but only in the extinguishment of consequential damages .
The jury shallreduce theirverdictto writing,and sign
the same ; and it,together with the warrant ,shallbe re-
turned by the sheriffto the clerk of the circuitcourt for
said county ,who shalldocket the same as other suits,giv-
ing it the precedenceof allcivilbusiness; of which the
owner , occupant , agent, attorney, or guardian shallhave

court to which the inquest is returned; and upon the

ingsaidinquisition,unlessdefensebe made and sufficient
cause be shown against it; and when so-confirmed,the

pany ; but if itbe set aside,the court may directanother

shalldescribethe land to be taken by metes and bounds ,
and the extent and duration of the interest in the same ;
and such damages ,thus found , when tendered or paid to
the owner ,or his or theiragent or attorneyor legal repre-
sentative,or to the clerk of said court,for the use of said
owner ,when he , she, or they may refuse to receive the
same ,or reside out of the county ,shall,ipsofucto,vest in
said company the rightto the land,or to the use of the
same ,to allintents and purposes , as fullyas if the same
had been acquiredby validconveyance from the owners
of the same .
§5 . Said company , after its organization, shall have
power to unite with any other railroad company now
constructed,or that may hereafter be constructed, upon

1.68 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

by thiscorporation.
§7 . The laborersupon said road,and persons injuredin
personor property,shallhave a firstlienupon the prop-
erty of said road priorto any mortgage .

sage .

CHAPTER 637 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of the county of Madison to take
stock in turupike roads in said county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court of the county of Madison ,
a majority of the justices of the peace of said county
being present and concurringtherein, be,and they are
hereby, authorizedand empowered to subscribestock,
forand on behalfof saidcounty,toallthe turnpikecom-
panieswhich have been orshallhereafterbe incorporated

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 169
by said county court,or by the Legislature of the State
of Kentucky : Provided, That said subscriptionshall not
exceed one thousand dollars for each mile of road within
said county ,and shallnot be paid over to the officersof
the company , except upon the completion of each mile,
or upon the court being satisfiedthat means are in the
hands of the company to insure itscompletion : Provided
further, That before the said county court exercise the
powers granted ,they shallsubmit to the voters of the said
county , at a regular election, the question whether said
voters are in favor of making the subscriptionherein con-
templated ; and ifthe affirmativeof said propositionshall
receive a majority of votes then cast, for and against it,
the said county court shallproceed to make the subscrip-
tion, and exercisethe powers herein provided for without
any furthersubmission to the said voters.
§2 . Said county court, a majority of the justices of
the peace of said county being present and concurring,
shall have power to levy a tax upon all the property in
said county subject to taxation for revenue and county
purposes,at the time of the said levy,sufficientforthe
purpose of paying forthe subscriptionof saidstock; said
tax to be collectedin the same manner ,and by the same
officers,under the same liabilitiesand penalties,that the
revenue taxes are now collected .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 26 , 1878.

meanor ,and the payment of saidfinewas made by John

of the jury fund of the county. Seventy dollarsof said
finewas remittedby the Governor,James B. McCreary ,
which amount so remitted by His Excellency,the Gov-

170 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ernor, the trusteeof thejury fund refusesto pay back

Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth

CHAPTER 639 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Chattaroi
Railway Company ," approved March 11th, 1873.
WHEREAS ,It is proposed to build a railroad from the
Ohio river,up the Big Sandy river,to Peach Orchard,a
distance of about forty miles; and whereas , the build-
ing of said road will develop a districtof great mineral
wealth ,increasing largely the value of lands,invitecap-
italand population, encourage manufacturing ,& c.,thus
increasing the wealth of the State, and adding to itsrev-
enues . The road may be built on either the West Vir-
ginia side or the Kentucky side, at about equal cost.
Those proposing to build the road are unwilling to un-
dertake the enterprisewithout local aid; the people
along the line have made liberaldonations,but never
has been done on the West Virginia side,so that Ken-
tucky is likelyto lose the road ,and see a cityspring up
at Conda ,in sightof her border ; and whereas , the build
ing of the road on the West Virginia side would be of
littlevalue to Kentucky , and ,at the same time,postpone
to a distantfuturethe buildingof a road up the Sandy
valleyinthisState. The road,ifbuiltin Kentucky ,will
be under an act,entitled"An actto incorporatethe Chat-
taroi Railway Company ;" now , therefore,to encourage

CHAPTER 660 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the city of Louisville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

shallhave fullpower to transferthe unexpended balances
now to the creditof the jail and Fulton streetimprove-
ment fund to the new work -house account , and expend
the same in the construction and furnishing the said new
work -house .
§2 . That the general council of said city shall have

dollars' worth of property in said citysubject to taxation

houses of refuge .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved March 29 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 661 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the CarrollCounty Fair Association.
WHEREAS , Certain individuals, as stockholders, have
been actingunder what they supposed was a legalchar-
ter,in the name of the CarrollCounty Fair Association,
recordedin deed-book No. 11,pages 207 and 208,Carroll
county clerk'soffice,and thereare some doubts as to the
legalityof said supposed charter.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky:
§ 1. That thecharterof the CarrollCounty Fair Asso-
ciation, as recorded in deed -book No. 11,pages 207 and
208,in the Carrollcounty clerk'soffice,be,and thesame
ishereby,declaredand made legal; and allcontractshere-
1

192 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
toforemade by and with said associationshallbe bind-
ing and valid as to the partiesto such contracts.
§2 . That said association is hereby constituted and
createda body-politicand corporate,by the name of the
CarrollCounty Fair Association; and shallhave allthe
rights,privileges, powers,and franchisesnamed in said
charter, as recorded in deed -book No. 11, pages 207 and
208,Carrollcounty clerk'soffice,with power to sue and
be sued in said name , to contract and be contracted
with ; to buy and sellnot exceeding sixty acres of land,
in Carroll county ,at any one time; to convey or mort-
gage saidland by orderof the majorityof the directors;
to hold personal property,not exceeding in value fifty
thousand dollars .
§3 . The officersof said associationshallbe elected by
the stockholders,voting by shares, each share counting
as one vote; and the said association,by vote of a ma-
jorityof itsdirectors,may increase the number of shares
of stock at any time to two thousand .
§ 4. Said associationmay hold its fairs at such times
as itmay appoint,in Carrollcounty,forthe exhibitionof
stock,agriculturalimplements, products,and such other
articlesas the directorsmay permitto be exhibited; and
for the trialof the speed of horses in running , trotting,
or pacing ; and it may allow its grounds to be used for
alllawful exhibitions,picnics,and pleasure parties.
§ 5. The directorsshallhave power to make such rules,
regulationsand by-laws,as they may deem forthe bene-
fitof the association , not inconsistent with the Constitu-
tion and laws of this State, and they shall have power
to enforcethe same . The president,vicepresident,and
each director,shallbe a conservatorof the peace,with
power to arrest, and cause to be arrested, alldisorderly
persons on the grounds of said association. They shall
have power to appoint as many police as they deem nec-
essary, who shallbe sworn faithfullyto perform their
dutiesas such,and shallhave power and authorityof
the constables of Carrollcounty ,while engaged as police
for said association. It shallbe the duty of the police
so appointed to arrestalldisorderlypersons , and such as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 193
violate the law , and take them before some magistrate,
to be dealt with according to law .
§ 6. The directorsshallhave power tolicensepeddlers
and sales of any articleson theirgrounds ; and to allow
shows and performancesthereon,and to restrainallsuch
persons who do not take a license from the association
for such purposes .
§7 . The entire property of the association shall be
liablefor all debts of the corporation; but in no event
shall the members ,or any member of the association,be
liable for the debts of the association . The association
shallbe incorporatedunder thisactfortwenty-fiveyears,
with power to selland dispose of their franchises and
powers under thisact; and ifthedirectorsdeem itpru-
dent,theymay change thevalue of thesharesof thestock
from one hundred dollarsto such an amount as they think
proper,and reduceor increasethe number of sharespro-
portionably. The old stock in the associationmay be
counted at such figuresas are consideredequitableby
the directors,when they make a change in the value of
the shares of stock .
§ 8. The provisionsof the charter,as recorded in deed-
book No. 11,pages 207 and 208, Carrollcounty clerk's
office,in so far as they are in conflictwith this act,are
hereby amended so as to harmonize with and become a
part of thisact.
§ 9. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 29, 1878.

dependent on the proceeds of the sale of theirtobacco
for money to pay theirtaxes,and being unable to dis-
pose it,without greatsacrifice,beforespringor summer ;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

dollars,be,and ishereby,given untilthe firstday of July

intothe StateTreasury forthe year 1877: Provided, The

CHAPTER 683 .

WHEREAS ,On the 23d day of March ,1870,in the Bath
circuitcourt,Frances Otis,of Bath county,was found to
be an idiotby a verdict and judgment of said court, and
G. W. Barnes was appointed her committee ; and where-
as ,the said G. W. Barnes continued to act as her said
committee ; and whereas ,at the March term , 1876, of the
Bath circuitcourt,said Frances Otis was again found to
be an idiotby a judgment of said court; and whereas ,
the law requiredthat an inquestshould be held every

five years ; and whereas , the Auditor of Public Accounts
refused to pay for the care,support, and maintenance of
said idiotfor the interregnum between March ,1875 , and
March ,1876,because of the inquesthaving expired; and
whereas , G. W. Barnes , as the committee of said idiot,
cared for,supported,and maintained said Frances Otis,
theidiotaforesaid,from March ,1875,up to March , 1876,
out of his own means ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant on the Treasuryfor
the sum of seventy-five dollars ($ 75) in favor of G. W.
Barnes ,committee of Frances Otis,a pauper idiotof Bath
county, Kentucky , for the support and maintenance of
said idiotforthe year 1875
§2 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 30 , 1878.

CHAPTER 705 .
AN ACT topreventbaseballplayingon Sunday inKenton county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any number of per-
sons to play,or assemble for the purpose of playing,or
witnessing any game of base ball, on any field, farm ,
common ,square,street,or other place,in the county of
Kenton , on Sunday or the ChristianSabbath .

1
§2 . That any persons so playing ,or assembling for the
purpose of playing , or of witnessing any game of base
ball,in saidcounty,on Sunday or the ChristianSabbath,
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and shallbe subject to
a fine of not less than ten nor more than fiftydollars
for each offense, to be recovered before any justice of
the peace or police judge in said county ,upon warrant
issued upon affidavitfiled.
§3 . That itshallbe the duty of the county attorney of
said county to prosecute all offendersunder this act, and
forhis services shall receive as compensation the same
fees now allowed said attorney under the laws forprose-
cuting misdemeanor cases; and the justice or judge and
constablesrendering servicesunder thisact shallbe enti-
tled to charge and collectthe same fees as they are,each,
now entitled to charge and collect in misdemeanor cases .
§ 4. That any person or persons convictedand fined
under this act,and failingto pay or replevy the same ,
shallbe committed and confinedinthe county jailsuch
a number of days,at two dollarsper day,as willequal
the amount of the fineimposed .
§ 5. The provisions of the foregoing sectionsshallnot
applytopersonsprofessingthe Jewish or Israelitishfaith,
who observea differentSabbath than the firstday of the
week .
§ 6. This act shalltake in ten days afteritspassage .
Approved April 2, 1878.

CHAPTER 718 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof schooldistrictNo. 35 , in Ohio county .
WHEREAS , The common school trustee in district No.
35 ,in Ohio county ,by mistake in reporting to the com-
mon school commissioner the census enumeration of chil-
dren in said districtfor school year ending June the 30th ,
1877,onlyreportedforty-sixchildrenof pupilage,instead
of fifty-eight,the number actuallyin the district,thereby
causinga deficitof the publicmoney drawn by saiddis-
trictof one dollarand ninetycents each fortwelve chil-
dren , amounting to the sum of twenty -two dollars and
eighty cents; and whereas ,the trusteein said districtem-
ployed a teacher to teach the public school forthe agreed
compensation of the public money due a districtof fifty-
eightchildrenof pupil age ; and by the aforesaidmis-
take the said districtonly drew for forty-six children;
now ,to remedy thismistake,and to compensate the teacher
who taught the school for the fullterm of fivemonths , as
requiredby law ,therefore,

262 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Superintendent of Public Instruction is
hereby authorized to draw his warrant on the Auditor for
the amount of the deficit- twenty-two dollarsand eighty
cents- in favorschoolcommissioner of Ohio county (to
be paid to teacher of districtNo. 35),to be paid out of
any unbonded surplus remaining to the credit of Ohio
county .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 2 , 1878.

CHAPTER 721 .
FAN ACT forthe benefitof John G. Saylor,of Harlan county .
WHEREAS ,It appears to the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that Michael Saylor, of
Harlan county , who was found to be a pauper lunatic
of said county by the judgment of the Harlan county
court at itsJune term, 1875; and whereas,said Saylor,
by his friends, made applicationfor admission in the
lunatic asylum at Lexington , and that the superintend-
ent of said asylum notifiedsaid Saylor that there was
not room in said asylum for said Michael Saylor, and
could not receive him ; and whereas ,John G. Saylor kept
said Michael Saylor twelve months after he was refused
admission in the asylum ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be,and he is,
directedto draw his warrant upon the Treasury for the
sum of seventy-fivedollars,out of any money not other-
wise appropriated,forkeeping said Michael Saylor twelve
months afterhe was adjudged to be a pauper lunatic.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 2, 1878 .
CHAPTER 722 .
AN ACT to authorizethe courtof claims of the county of Grant to levy an
ad valorem tax .
aWHEREAS , The Grant county court have erected
bridge across Eagle creek, at Downingville , and have
thereby incurred a considerable debt against the said
county, which is unpaid ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thecounty courtof saidcounty,composed of
the judge thereofand a majorityof the justicesof the
peace ,be , and they are hereby , authorized and empow-
ered to levy on propertylistedfor taxationfor revenue
purposesan ad valorem tax ofnot exceedingfifteencents

264 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
on each one hundred dollars for each year said tax is
levied; and the said court is authorized to levy the said
tax from year to year untilthe whole debt,incurred by
the constructionof said bridge,isdischarged .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffof said county to
collectand pay over the tax levied under thisact under
the orders of said court ; and he and his sureties on his
bond forthe collectionof the county levy shallbe liable
for his failureto perform his duties under thisact.
§ 3. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 2, 1878.
CHAPTER 723 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to provide for the erectionof
schoolbuildingsin common schooldistrictNo. 1, in Hawesville,Hancock
county ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itis the true intent and meaning of sections
fiveand twelve of said act,that all propertyof every
description,which is subject to taxation for State reve.
nue,shallbe subjectto taxationforthe purposesof said
act .
§2 . That the assessorauthorized to be appointed by
the act to which this is an amendment shall return his
liston or before the 15th day of May in each year , and
the board of education shall have power by themselves,
or commissionersappointed forthe purpose,to supervise
and correct any errors,delinquencies, or omissions ; and
for that purpose shallhave fullpower to swear and ex-
amine witnesses,and make such correctionsas are just
and equitable .
§3 . That the collectorsof taxes shallhave allthe pow-
ersnow given by law to the sheriffsin collectingthe State
revenue , afteradvertisingat the court-house door in said
town the factthatthelisthas been placed in hishands
for collection; and if the same isnot paid within sixty
days thereafter,he may proceed to levy on any property
of those delinquentthatissubjectto levyby the sheriff

for State taxes; and he shall,within four months after
receiving the tax-list,collectand pay over to the treas-
urer of the board the whole amount of taxes collectable,
unless the time shall be extended by the board . Upon
his failureto do so,he may be proceeded against by mo-
tion in the circuitcourt, after ten days' notice by the
board of education .

and the trustees of the common schools in said district,
which shall be known as the Board of Education of said
town and district,in any year failto levy the taxes con-
templated and authorized by sectionsfive and twelve of
the act to which this is an amendment ,for the purposes
therein setforth,itshallbe lawful forthe presiding judge

court,upon the petition of any citizen of the district,to

and to that end he ishereby invested with allthe author-
ityconferredby law on thesaid board. Said taxesshall
be paid out and appropriated by him as directed in the

§5 . That the board of education shall have fullcon-
trol and management of the schools authorized by the

er to make such rules and laws for theirgovernment as

of the State of Kentucky ; that they may prescribe the

the school and classes, as they may think necessary, to
give the full benefits of a high school education to all
those entitledto tuitionherein; prescribe the qualifica-
tion of teachersand pupilsin said schools; fixthe sal-
ariesof teachers and other employes ; dismiss or suspend
any teacher employed by them ; and to establishrates
and terms on which childrenresidingout of said district
may be admitted to said school.
§ 6. That so much of the act to which this is an amend-
ment , as conflictswith thisact,ishereby repealed.

.

CHAPTER 726 .
AN ACT to amend the citycharterof Owensboro .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That in lieu of the firstMonday of April,for the
biennial election of a cityand police judge and a city
marshal of Owensboro , the election to fillsaid offices
shallbe holden in said cityon the firstMonday of Au-
gust, at a regular State election, and that the firstelection
under this act shallbe in August ,A. D. 1880,and every
two years thereafter. All acts and parts of said charter
inconsistentherewith are repealed.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom the firstday of Janu-
ary, 1880; and every incumbent of said officeof cityand
policejudge, duly electedthereafter, shall possessthe
same qualificationsprescribedby law for a circuitjudge
of this Commonwealth .
Approved April 2, 1878.

childrento the Superintendent of Public Instructionforthe
school year ending June 30th, 1876,inadvertentlyomitted
thenames of ten (10) pupil childrenreportedto him by
the trustee of districtNo. 51 , and it is desirable that said
districtshould not suffer on account of said error ; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

(30)centsbe appropriatedtosaiddistrict,tobe paid out of
any surplus interestdue said county on itssurplus bond
forthe schoolyears ending June 30th,1877,and June 30th,

urer for the above amount .

CHAPTER 733 .
AN ACT ,entitled"An act to authorizethe constablesin the county of Hen-
derson to collectthe delinquenttaxes forthe years 1873, 1874, and 1875."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the several constables
in the county of Henderson to collectthe delinquent State
revenue taxes and county leviesdue by the tax-payers
of saidcounty fortheyears 1873,1874,and 1875; and for
that purpose they are hereby directed to receive the lists
of said delinquenttax-payers, and they shallhave the
same power to enforce said collectionby levy and sale
that the sheriffhas by law to collecttaxes; and they

272 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shall be responsible upon their officialbonds for all
money collectedby them under thisact.
§2 . That John M. Johnson ,late sheriffof Henderson
county ,or any person having the tax-books for said years
in charge ,shall furnishto each constable of said county
a copy of so much of said books as show the taxes for
said years to be unpaid .
§3 . Said constables are allowed to retain fifteenper
cent. of the amount collectedby them respectively, and
the balance they shall pay to the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts,or to such person as he may ,in writing, authorize
to receive the same ; and said amounts when paid shall
be allowed as creditsupon the judgment heretofore ob-
tained by the Commonwealth against John M. Johnson ,
late sheriff, and his sureties; but nothing herein shall in
anywise affect the prosecution of any suit which has
been or may be institutedto enforce said judgment , or
to collect the amount said Johnson and his sureties are
bound for .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 2, 1878.

CHAPTER 735 .
AN ACT for the benefitof J. P. Cooper,committee for Malinda F. White ,
pauper idiot.
WHEREAS , The circuitcourt of Allen county failed to
have an inquestheld,at the end of fiveyears afterthe
firstinquest was held,to inquire into the stateof mind ,
& c.,of Malinda F. White ,a pauper idiot,on the second
day of January , 1877, and did not hold any inquest until
the twenty -ninth day of August , 1877, which leaves a
period of six months and twenty-seven days for which
said Malinda F. White had no allowance forher support ;
and whereas ,the Auditor has refused to pay her commit-
tee for the time for which the court failed to have the
inquest renewed ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of PublicAccounts be,and he is
hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasurer for
the sum of forty-three dollarsand thirteencents in favor
of J.P. Cooper,committee forMalinda F. White ,a pau-
per idiot, being amount due for her support from the
second day of January , 1877, untilthe twenty -ninth day
of August , 1877 .
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 2 , 1878.

CHAPTER 739 .
AN ACT forthe benefitofL. B. Rose,J.M. Rose,Martha A. Rose,Betty
Gaines Rose , and Martha Ellen Rose .
WHEREAS , Farthey Catherine Rose departed thislife,in
Marshall county ,about the year 1874, leaving an estate
in said county , which descended to her only child, a bas
tard,to-wit,Mary E. Rose ; and soon thereaftersaidMary
E. Rose departed this life,without issue, and said estate
by law escheats to the Commonwealth ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Commonwealth of Kentucky releasesall
interest in said estate, and the same shall descend and be
distributed to L. B. Rose ,J. M. Rose , Martha A. Rose ,
Betty Gaines Rose ,and Martha Ellen Rose ,in the same
proportion and likemanner as if the said Mary E. Rose
had been a legitimatechildof her mother ,Farthey Cath-
erine Rose .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 2, 1878.

CHAPTER 759 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Sidney Hunter , a pauper idiot.
WHEREAS ,It appears that no inquest was held in the
caseof Sidney Hunter,a pauper idiotof Madison coun-
ty,in May .1875,as requiredby law ; and itfurtherap-
pearing that an inquest was held in September , 1875,
thereby making an interregnum of four months, which
the Auditor was not authorized to pay ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby , directed to draw his warrant on the Treasury
in favorof Jephthah Johnson,committee forSidneyHun-
ter,forthe sum of twenty -five dollars,itbeing forthe said
interregnum of four months .
§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 3, 1878.
CHAPTER 760 .

of Kentucky :

orders, or other evidences of indebtedness against the
Cabin Creek ,Sand Hill, and Manchester Turnpike Road
Company ,be requiredto produce saidevidences of in-
debtedness to the county court of Lewis county , on three

county ,by posting a written notice on the front door of

notice in the Vanceburg Courier and Maysville Bulletin
for at least three months . Said evidences of indebted-
ness,when produced,shallbe registeredupon the record-
book of said court, and any person failingto produce

CHAPTER 761 .
AN ACT for the benefitof C. W. Wantland ,jailerof Simpson county .
WHEREAS , On the day of January last,John Banton
escaped from the jail of Simpson county , where he had
been confined by the examining court, in default of
$ 1,200bail, for the offenseof cruellyand dangerously
beatingand robbingHumphrey Hammond ;and whereas,
the escape was withoutthe faultof thejailer,but in con-
sequence of the inherent defect of thejail; and whereas ,
the jailer, C. W. Wantland , in order to stimulate and
encourage extraordinary effortto capture said Bunton ,
promptly offereda reward of $200 forhiscapture,with-
out waiting to learn whether the Governor would offera
reward or not; and whereas ,said Bunton was captured in
the State of Illinois, above St. Louis , on the Illinoisside
of the Missipppi river, and has been returned and is now
in the jail of Simpson county - the said Bunton having
confessed to the crime as above setout, and testifiedalso
against his accomplice in the examining court,whose tes-
timony was and is necessary to the conviction of his said
accomplice; and whereas, the said Wantland has paid
the aforesaid reward of $ 200,together with the sum of
$ 101 25 as expenses ,which sums he had to borrow , and
pledge his fees to secure,being a poor man with large
family and a faithfulofficer; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditorof PublicAccounts be,and he is
hereby ,directed to draw his warrant on the Treasury in
favor of C. W. Wantland , for the sum of two hundred
dollars .
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 3, 1878.

CHAPTER 775 .
AN ACT to establisha public school for childrenof the African race in
the city of Paducah .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the trusteesof the public school of the cityof
Paducah shallestablish,organize, and maintain a public
school for children of the African race residing in the
said city, which shall be known as the Colored Public
School of Paducah .
§2 . The trusteesof the public school shallexercise a
general supervision over the said colored public school,
appointteachers,make rulesand regulations,adopt text
books and a course of studyfor the same ,and appoint,
within twenty days from the passage of thisact, fivedis-
creet qualifiedcolored voters in the city,who shallbe the
advisory board of trusteesof the colored public school.
§3 . The trusteesof the publicschoolshallprescribe
the duties of the colored trustees, make rules and regula-
tions for theirgovernment ,appoint teachers and remove
them forcause,and supplyallvacanciesin thesaidadvi-
sory board caused by death , resignation, removal from
office, or removal from the city.
§4 . The funds for the support of the coloredpublic
school shallconsistof the pro rata from the State forthe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 315
education of colored childrenresidingin the cityof Padu-
cah , all taxes collectedfrom the colored persons of said
cityunder the provisionsofthe citycharterforfiveyears
from the firstday of January,1878.and such otherfunds
as are or may be set apart by law ,donation ,or otherwise,
for the use and benefitof the said colored public school.
§5 . The fund provided for in thisact shallbe kept sep-
arate,collectedand disbursed as the funds for the support
of public schools of thisState now are or may hereafter
be .
§6 . To enable the trustees of the public school to
provide,by purchase or otherwise,suitable grounds and
buildingsfor the colored school of Paducah ,the trustees
of said public school shallhave the right,if in their dis-
cretion they may deem it proper ,to issue the bonds of
said colored public school in an amount not exceeding
five thousand dollars, to bear any rate of interest not
exceeding eightper cent.per annum ,in such amounts as
they may see proper ,the bonds to be signed by the pres-
ident and secretaryof the trusteesof the common schools
of Paducah . Said bonds , if issued, may be sold by the
trusteesof the common schools for money ,or may be ex-
changed for real estate on which to locate said colored
public school, and for labor in the erection of school
buildingson thesame . Said bonds may be made payable
at such times as the trusteesof the common schools may
thinkproper. To saidbonds may be attachedcoupons to
represent the intereston said bonds ,payable half yearly
at some bank in the cityof Paducah ; the coupons to be
signed by the clerk of the trustees of the common schools
alone .
§7. This actshalltake effectthirtydays afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 5, 1878.

AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of spirituous,vinous, or malt liquorswithin
two miles of South Union or Hebron Churches, in the southern part of
Christian county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to sell
any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,at any place with-
in two miles of South Union Church or Hebron Church ,
in the southern part of Christian county .
§2 . That any person violatingthe provisionsof thisact
shallbe subjectto the penaltiesand punishments which
are imposed by statuteupon persons guiltyof retailing
and tippling.
§3 . This act shall take effecton the firstday of May ,
1878 .
Approved April 5, 1878.

CHAPTER 793 .
AN ACT to enable the presidentand directorsto sellthe Eminence and
SmithfieldTurnpike Road .
WHEREAS , Henry White , Orvil Ford ,James Omea , Jas.
Callaway , Compton Crabb ,presidentand directorsof the
Eminence and Smithfield Turnpike Road Company , a
company organized under the general turnpike law of
Henry county ,having advanced the sum of twelve hund-
red dollars of their own means for the construction of
said turnpike road,and there being no means of the com-
pany , or any subscriptionswith which to repay them ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the said president and directorsare empow-
ered to institutean action in equity in the Henry circuit
court,to subject said road and all its franchises to the
payment of said debt, interests, and cost. All the stock-
holders must be made partiesto the action,and may
plead any defensetouching the validityof the claim set
up . Should the road be sold,the purchaser is vested
with all the rights of the originaldirectors and stock-
holders,the road to be appliedto the likeuses,and the
publicto have the rightto pass over the same ,subjectto
the provisionsof the general turnpike law of the county .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 5, 1878.

ally,on the firstSaturday in February , at such place as

§6 That at the annual election there shall be elected ,

the highest number of votes in the whole town for either
of said offices shall be declared elected .
§7 . That the officerselectedby the people under the
provisions of this act shallrespectivelyhold their offices
for one year and until their successors are elected and
qualified respectively. All other officersmentioned in
thisact, and not otherwise specificallyprovided for,may
be appointed by the board of trusteesat the firstregular
meeting thereof after the annual election, or as soon
thereafteras may be,and respectivelycontinue in office

may be appointed to hold officeduring the pleasure of
the board of trustees .

under the laws of the State; and no one shallbe eligible
to the officeof policejudge ,trustee, marshal ,or attorney,
who has not , at the time of his election, resided in said
town at leastsix months previous to such election.

under the provisions of thisact,before entering upon the
duties thereof,shalltake and subscribe,before some offi-
cer authorized to administer oaths,the oath of officepre-
scribedby the Constitutionof thisCommonwealth , and

thereon, and to enforce full compliance therewith, as

rants and other process,in the name of the Common-

judgment and award , and issue writs for the purpose of
enforcing its orders and judgments , to the same extent,
and in as fulla manner , as other courts of this Common-
wealth of likejurisdiction.
§32 . That said court shall have jurisdictionconcur-
rent with justices of the peace of Pendleton county in

tresswarrants ,and warrants in forcibleentry and detainer

of courtswith likejurisdictionand powers .

tokeep thepeace,the power of two justicesof the peace
in holding examining courts,to issue subpœnas for wit-
nesses,and to orderthe marshal to summon juries,and
do allotheractsand thingsnecessaryand propertocarry

334 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
intofulland complete effectthe powers and jurisdiction

failingto designate and provide a suitableplace,at such

cess of said court shall be entitled to the same fees for

§ 40. That thedefendantshallhave therightto replevy
forthreemonths any judgment ,fine,or forfeitureof the

policecourt,on executionor otherfinalprocess thereon,
by giving bond , with good security,in the same manner
as allowed by law in similarcaseswithinthejurisdiction
of other courts of this Commonwealth .

nually levy and collecttaxes,not exceeding one dollar

CHAPTER 799 .
AN ACT to incorporate" The Board of Trusteesforthe Graded City School
of Cynthiana, Kentucky ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That A. J. Beale ,T. A. Frazer ,A. W. Lydick ,R.
M. Martin , H. McDowell , W. V. Prather, Luther Van-
hook , and R. C. Wherritt, citizensof Cynthiana , Ken-
tucky,and at thispresentthe duly constitutedboard of
trusteesfor the graded cityschool of said city,and their
successorsin office,be,and they are hereby declared to
be ,a body-corporateand politic,with perpetualsucces-
sion,by the name and styleof " The Board of Trustees
fortheGraded CitySchoolof Cynthiana,Kentucky ;" and
as such,by that name shall be capable in law of con-
tractingand being contracted with,suing and being sued,
in all courts and places,and in all matters whatsoever .
The absolute and unconditional control of the graded
city school for said city is hereby vested in said board
of trustees; and the said board are hereby empowered to
do all those things necessary and proper to be done to
secure the successfulmanagement thereof,with fullpower
and authorityto buy ,sell,hold, or convey ,in theircorpo-
rate capacity,any estate,eitherreal,personal,or mixed.
§2 . No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof trustee,
except he be a white male,residentof the cityof Cyn-
thiana, and over twenty -one years of age .
§3 . The term of officeof each member of the school
board shall be four years from the date of their elec-
tion; and whenever vacanciesoccur,by expirationof the
term of officeor otherwise,the board shallorder an elec.
tionto supplythe same ; said electionsto be held at the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 345
same time ,under the supervisionof the same officersof
election,and in the same manner as the cityelectionsfor
saidcityof Cynthianaare now held: Provided,That such
vacancies as occur by resignation,death ,or otherwise than
by expirationof term of office,may be temporarilyfilled
by the board of trustees,upon itsown motion or election.
§4. The officersof saidboard shallconsistof a presi-
dent, vice president, clerk, treasurer, attorney, and col-
lector. The president,vice president,clerk,and collector
of said board shall be electedby said board at the first
regularmeetingthereofin May ,1878. The presidentand
vice president thereof shall,each of them ,be members of
the board of trustees . The treasurer of the board of trus-
tees shall be the same as the treasurer of the city of Cyn-
thiana,electedat the same time,in the same manner ,and
shallhold his officeforthe same period as now prescribed
by the charterof said city. He shall,before enteringupon
the discharge of his duties,execute bond , with good and
sufficientsureties, to be approved by the board,for the
faithfulperformance of his duties. He shall keep all
funds which may come into his hands ,for the use or the
benefit of the board of trustees aforesaid,separate and
distinctfrom all other funds, and shall make a monthly
statement of his accounts to the board of trustees . The
clerk of the board shall be elected as aforesaid for the
period of one year, and shallhold his officeuntilhis suc-
cessor is elected and qualified, and shall receive for his
servicessuch compensation as the board may fixor deter-
mine upon . The board shall have power to fixand de-
termine the duties of each and every one of its officers,
and to remove any of their officersor members for any
gross misconduct or neglect of duty ,by a two -thirdsvote
of the whole board . The board may make and alter, at
their pleasure, a set of by -laws for theirgovernment :
Provided, That the same be not in conflictwith any of
the provisionsof thisact.
§ 5. The " board of trusteesfor the graded cityschool
of Cynthiana,Kentucky," shallhave the power and au-
thorityin each year to levyand collectan ad valorem

346 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tax on allthe propertyin the cityof Cynthiana.belong.
ing to white persons,subjectto taxation,not exceeding
fifty(50) centson each one hundred dollars'($ 100) worth
of such assessedproperty. The tax listshallbe made
out by the clerk of the board of trustees from the city
assessor'slistor report, as made to the common council
of said cityin each year . He shallarrange the said list
alphabetically, and set down therein the name of each
tax-payer , and the aggregate value of the taxable prop-
erty listedby the same ,and opposite thereto the amount
of tax due thereon,at the rateor per centage as fixed by
the board for that year . The listas so made out shallbe
placed in the hands of the treasurerof the board for col-
lection,and the taxes for the use and benefit of said board
of trustees shall be collected in the same manner as other
taxes,by law ,are collectedin said city,exceptthatsuch
tax-payers as shallnot pay to the treasurer of the board
the respectiveamounts of taxes owing by them to the
said board in each year ,on or before a day certain to be
fixed and advertised by said board at least fifteendays
prior thereto, shall be declared delinquent tax-payers.
The said board may then order a listof said delinquent
tax-payers to be made out by the clerk,and placed in the
hands of the collectorof the board ,who shallhave power
and authorityto collectthe same ,together with a penalty
ofnot exceedingten (10) per cent.added. If such delin-
quents do not pay the amount of tax owing by them to
the collectoron or before the firstday of November in
each year ,together with allcosts and penaltiesthereon,
then,at any time aftersaid date,the collectormay pro-
ceed to collectthe same by levy, distraint, attachment ,or
garnishment ,in the same manner as by law sheriffsare
directed to collecttaxes; and chapter 92 of the General
Statutes, articles8 and 9 thereof, are hereby adopted and
made a part of thisact of incorporation,so far as itspro-
visionsin the directionto sheriffswillapply to thecollec-
tor of the board in the collection of taxes within the city
of Cynthiana .

CHAPTER 805 .
AN ACT to prevent swine from running at largein the cityof Frankfort.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the mayor and councilmen of the city of
Frankfort be , and are hereby , empowered to pass an
ordinance or ordinances declaring it unlawful for swine
of any age to run at large within said city, and to fix
and have imposed such fine or punishment for each of-
fense as they may deem proper , and as they may now
have the power to cause to be inflictedfor any breach of
the peace within said city.
§2 . Said mayor and councilmen may cause the mar-
shal,any deputy marshal,watchman or policemanof said
city,to take up and impound ,in a placeprovidedforthat
purpose,any swine so running at largein saidcity,and
advertise , or cause to be advertised , the fact that such
swine had been taken up and impounded ,forthe space of
ten days ,by posting up ,at at least four public or promi-
nent places in said city,a written or printed copy of such
advertisement; said advertisement must contain a descrip-
tion, as nearly as it can well be given , of the age ,color,
and ear-marks (iftherebe any),of such swine,thetime
and place the same willbe sold, if not sooner calledfor
and reclaimedby the owner ,by hisprovingpropertyand
paying allcostsand expensesfortakingup and keeping
said swine . The salethus made by the marshal ,or other
properofficerof saidcity,shallpass a completetitleand
ownership in and to such propertyto the purchaser,as
much so as a sale made by a sheriffat a sheriff'ssale
does to the purchaserat such sale: Provided, That such

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 355
salebe made at the citypound or otherpublicplace,by
publiclycryingor auctioneeringsuch propertyoffto the
highestand best bidder: And providedfurther, That,be-
fore such saleis made ,the propertyto be sold must be
valuedby two housekeepers,who shallbe firstsworn ,by
some officerauthorized to administer an oath,that they
willfaithfullydischargetheirdutiesas such appraisers.
§3 . The said mayor and councilmen may ,in addition
to punishing,or causing to be punished, the owners of
stray swine ,pass an ordinance inflictinga fine,of not to
exceed twenty -five dollars,for lettingout of said pound
any swine impounded therein,or forotherwiseinterfer-
ing with the enforcement of thisact,or of any ordinance
passed in pursuance of thisact.
§4 . Any money resultingfrom a sale made as above
provided, shallbe paid into the citytreasury, and the
owner or owners of such swine so sold may , within one
year next aftersuch sale,demand and have paid back to
him or them allthe proceeds of such sale,afterdeducting
allthe costsof such takingup ,impounding ,keeping,ad-
vertising,and selling; said costs to include a fee of one
dollar to the officerso impounding , advertising, and sell-
ing .
§5 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 5, 1878.

CHAPTER 819 .

of saidcountyto aidin theconstructionof turnpikeroads
in said county ; and whereas ,said court of claims,since
the passage of said act,have made subscriptionsto various
turnpike companies in saidcounty ,and in payment of said
subscriptionshave made and issued to said companies
bonds , in pursuance of the provisions of said act; and
whereas ,under and by virtue of an act of the General
Assembly of Kentucky ,approved February 1st,1868,and
an act amendatory thereof,approved March 7th, 1870,a
majority of the qualifiedvoters of said county , at a legal
election,voted in favor of an annual tax of eight cents
on each hundred dollarsof assessedtaxablepropertyin
said county ,to be used for the construction of turnpike
roads in said county ; and whereas ,there has been col-
lected,by the sheriffsof said county ,foreach of the years
1868 and 1869 ,a tax of five cents on each hundred dollars,

counted forand paid over to said court by said sheriffs,
have been applied,from year toyear,under the ordersof
said court,to the payment of the accrued interestupon

teredof recordany formalordersdirectingthe levyand
collection of said taxes ; therefore ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 365
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

and issuingsaid bonds in payment of the subscriptionsof

the same ishereby ,legalized,and the said bonds are made
and declared to be as valid and binding as if said court
had , from year to year , made orders of record, levying
and directing the collectionof said tax for the payment
of the principal and interestof said bonds .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage : Provided,
That nothing in this act shall be construed as affecting the
question of the liabilityof the suretiesin the officialbond
or bonds of any sheriffor sheriffswho may have made
defaultin the payment of said collections.

CHAPTER 822 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Drs.Smith and Crawford,of Nelson county.
WHEREAS ,It is shown to the satisfaction of this General
Assembly that Sam . Ford , who is now confined in the
penitentiary, was ,at the October term in the Nelson cir-
cuit court,triedand convictedon two separatecharges,
one for rescuing prisoners charged with murder , and the
other for grand larceny ; and that,whilst being conveyed
from Bardstown to the penitentiary,attempted to escape

370 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and was shot,and was so dangerouslywounded by the
guard as to require the constant care and attention of
physicians; and it appearing that Dr A. Crawford at-
tended said Ford from the 7th of November , 1877, until
the 2d of December of the same year ,making from three
to fivevisitsper day,and that Dr. A. Smith visitedsaid
Ford in consultation with Dr. Crawford ,from the 7th of
November until the 24th of the same month ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

hereby,directedto issuehiswarrant on the Treasurerin
favor of saidA. Crawford, for the sum of ninety-seven
dollars, and in favor of said A. Smith , for the sum of
thirty-fivedollars,to be paid out of any money in the
Treasury not otherwise appropriated.
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 6, 1878 .

CHAPTER 834 .
AN ACT for the benefitof George W. Hunter ,of Bardstown ,Nelson county.
WHEREAS , His Excellency,James B. McCreary ,as Gov-
ernor of Kentucky ,did, on the 19th day of April, 1877,
offera reward forthe captureof Grove Kennedy ,a fugi-
tive from justice; and whereas , said reward had ,on or
about the 1st day of September , 1877, failed to induce
any person or persons to undertake the arrest of said
Kennedy ; and whereas, the Governor had been peti-
tioned by sundry citizensof Garrard and Lincoln coun-
tiesto send State militiato said counties for the purpose
of arresting said Kennedy ; and whereas ,the Governor ,
aftergivingthe matter due consideration,and being of
the opinion that said arrestcould be effectedat less ex-
pense to the State than by the employment of the State
militia,did request George W. Hunter , of Nelson county ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 377
Kentucky , to effectsaid arrest; and whereas ,said George
W. Hunter did undertake to and did arrestsaid Kennedy
on the 27th day of October , 1877; and whereas ,the ex-
penses of said Hunter , and those who assistedin that
arrestand delivery of said Kennedy to the State author-
ity, was greatly in excess of the reward offeredby the
Governor ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of the State of Kentucky be , and
and he is hereby , directed and authorized to draw his
warrant on the State Treasurer in favor of George W.
Hunter , for the sum of four hundred dollars,to be paid
out of any money not otherwise appropriated, which
shall be in full of all claims of said Hunter , and those
who assistedin the arrest of said Kennedy ,for the ser-
vices rendered in said capture.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 6, 1878 .

CHAPTER $ 38 .
AN ACT to releasesheriffsof Green and Taylor countiesfrom collecting
railroadtaxes,and forother purposes.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter the sheriffsof Green and Taylor
counties shall not be required to give bond for the col-
lectionof any levyor tax in said countiesfor the pur-
pose of paying the principal or intereston the county
bonds of said counties issued for railroad purposes , and
shall not be held responsible in their,or either of their,
official bonds for the same .
§2 . That the county courts of said counties shall, at
the instance or motion of any person interested, appoint
a special collectorto collectall taxes or levies in said
counties forrailroadpurposes. Said appointee shallhave
all the qualificationsof sheriff, and have allthe powers
of sheriff to enforce the collection of said taxes ; and
shall give bond , with security, to be approved by the
county court, forthe faithfuldischarge of allduties im-
posed by reason of said appointment of special collector
of said railroad taxes .
§3 . Alllaws and actsor partsof actsin conflictwith
this act are hereby repealed.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
[Became a law withoutthesignatureof theGovernorApril9, 1878.]

CHAPTER 861 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof ProfessorT. W. Tobin , of Central University,
Richmond , Kentucky .
WHEREAS ,ProfessorT. W. Tobin,of CentralUniversity,
Richmond ,Kentucky,has inventeda scientificapparatus
calleda Sine Pendulum ,which is of great value to science
in determining the rotary motion of the earth; and where-
as, said Tobin desires to exhibit said Sine Pendulum at
the Paris Exposition; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sum of two hundred and fiftydollars is
hereby appropriated to enable said Tobin to perfect the
mechanism of said apparatus ,and have the same trans-
ported to Paris,France .
§ 2. That the Auditor of Public Accounts is hereby di-
rectedtodraw hiswarrant on the TreasurerofKentucky ,
in favorof Bennett H. Young ,one of Kentucky's Com-
missioners to the Exposition aforesaid,for two hundred
and fiftydollars,the same to be paid out of any money
not otherwise appropriated,and to be used by said Young
forthe purposes aforesaid under the directionof said T.
W. Tobin .
§3 . That thisSine Pendulum ,when made and exhibited
at Paris and brought back to thiscountry ,shallbelong to
the State of Kentucky ,and commissioner Young ischarged
with the duty of bringing said instrument back to this
country and deliveringthe same to the Secretary of State.
§4 . That this act shall take effectfrom and after its
passage .
Approved April 8 ,1878.

McClean ,C. W. West ,R. W. Ridgely,O. Kinnard, W. H.
