Wittman ,John B. Stevens ,T. W. Anderson ,James W.
Lacy,and H. W. Turtoy are now liableby law to work

road over that part of the said Falmouth road which

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 403

now , therefore ,

thiana, to the foot of the hillbeyond the intersectionof

CHAPTER 866 .
AN ACT to preventhorses,mules,cattle,goats,sheep, swine,geese,or ducks
from running at largeand trespassingin districtNo. 1, Harrison county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any person or per-
sons having in charge ,or being the owners of any horses,
mules ,cattle,sheep ,goats,swine ,geese ,or ducks , to suf-
ferthesame to run at largein any publicroad,highway ,
or in any street,lane, alley, or upon any uninclosed land
in districtNo. 1,in Harrisoncounty,or cause such ani-
mals to be herded ,kept ,or detained for the purpose of
grazing the same on premises other than those owned or
occupiedby theowner or keeper of such animals,except
as hereinafterprovided ; and any person violatingthe pro-
visionsof this act shall forfeitand pay , for every such
violation, as penalty therefor,not lessthan one dollar or
more than fivedollars. Continued violation, afternotice
or prosecution,shallbe heldto be an additionaloffense
for each and every day of such continuance .
§2 . That allsuitsto recover the penaltiesprovided for
in thisact shallbe brought in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky,on complaintof any person feeling
aggrieved,before any justiceof the peace or other court
having jurisdictionwhere the offenseis committed; and
the partyoffendingshall,on conviction,pay the amount
of penaltiesadjudged,with costs. All moneys collected
as penaltiesby virtueof thisact shallbe paid intothe
treasuryof thecounty forthe use of the common schools
of said county .
§3 . That no prosecutionshallbe commenced torecover
the penaltiesnamed in the firstsectionof thisact until

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 405
at least one day's notice shall have been given to the
owners or persons having charge of such animals , as the
case may be ; and it shallbe sufficientdefense to such
prosecution to show that such animal was at large with-
out the knowledge of such owner or keeper and without
fault of his .
§4 . That the owner ,or any person having control of
any animal describedin thisact,allowing the same to run
at large in violation of this act, shall be liable for dam-
ages done by said animals upon the premises of another ,
without reference to the fence which may inclose said
premises: Provided, Nothing in thisact shallbe so con-
strued as render the owner or keeper of any of the ani-
mals mentioned in the first section of this act liable for
any damages arisingto any railroad.
§ 5. That any person finding any animals mentioned in
thisact at large contrary to itsprovisions,may , and any
sheriff, constable, or marshal of any city,shall take up
and confine the same forthwith, giving notice thereof to
the owner ,ifknown ,and ifnot known ,by posting notices
describingsuch animals therein in at least three public
places within the district; and if the owner does not ap-
pear and claim his property,and pay allthe charges for
taking up , advertising, and keeping the same ,within ten
days from the date of said notice,the said animals may
be proceeded with under the law then in force regulating
estrays .
§6 . That the plural number used in this act naming
the animals to be prohibitedfrom running at large, and
the word "animals," whenever used herein,shallbe under-
stood and held to apply as well in the singular as in the
pluralnumber , and the word " person" and persons"
shallapply to both natural and artificialpersons.
،،
§7 . That the person or officertaking up such animals
shallbe entitledto charge and receive,from the owner of
such animals ,the following fees,in addition to those au-
thorized by the law regulating estrays,to-wit : for taking
up and advertisingeach animal of the horse or mule kind,
one dollar; each head of neat cattle,seventy-five cents ;

405 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each swine,fiftycents; each sheep,goose,or duck,twen-
ty-fivecents, and also reasonablepay for keeping the
same .
§ 8. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed.
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1878.

ing of such houses and the sale of ardent spirits. Said

shal by law,and to be approved by said court,forthe
faithfulperformance of the dutiesof saiddeputy or dep-
uties; and said marshal may revoke said appointments at

CHAPTER 875 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Woodson Trisler,a pauper idiot.
WHEREAS ,It appears that no inquest was held in the
case of Woodson Trisler, a pauper idiotof Jessamine
county,in May ,1875,as requiredby law ; and itfurther
appearing that an inquest was held in March , 1876,
thereby making an interregnum of ten months ,which the
Auditor was not authorized to pay ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts is authorized
and directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor
of Aaron Smith , committee for Woodson Trisler,for the
sum of sixty-two dollars and fiftycents,itbeing for the
said interregnum of ten months .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 8, 1878.

of Kentucky :

ury, or account for all taxes for the year 1877 for which
he is bound . The said sheriff is not entitled to the
benefitof thisact untilhis securitiesacknowledge them-
selvesstillbound ,which shallbe made a matter of record
as now requiredby law .
§2 . That this act take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 8 , 1878.

CHAPTER 587 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled " An act for the benefit of school district
No. 39,in Bath county," approved February 9th, 1876 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That chapter 134 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , entitled"An
act for the benefit of school district No. 39 , in Bath
county," approved February 9, 1876, be, and the same
is hereby ,so amended that all the qualifiedwhite male
voters of said district, and white widows residing in said
district,who have children between the ages of six and
twenty years,shall have the privilege of voting for the
trustees of said Sharpsburg Male and Female College;
the said widows having the right to vote either in per-
son or by written proxy .
§2 . That no person shall be a qualifiedvoter at said
election for trustees who shall not have been a bona fide
resident of said school districtNo 39 for at least sixty
days before said election; and that no one , whatever
may be the amount of stock he may hold in said build-
ing,shall have the privilege of casting more than one
vote for each of five trustees .
§3 . That the election for said trustees shall be held on
the firstSaturday in June , 1878,and annually thereafter
upon theday upon which the trusteesof common schools
are requiredto be electedby the common schoollaw of
the State .
§4 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 8,1878.

CHAPTER 889 .
AN ACT in relationto second-hand and "junk " storesin the cityof Lou-
isville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Every person who shall carry on the business of
buying and sellingsecond -hand goods ,merchandise ,wares ,
or materials of whatsoever description,shallbe deemed a
second -hand dealer within the meaning of this act .
§2 It shallbe the duty of the owner or proprietor of
every second -hand or "junk " establishment to whom a
license is issued to carry on said business,in the city of
Louisville, to enter into bond , with surety in an amount
to be fixed and approved by the citycouncil,to faithfully
perform and observe allthe requirements of thisact, and
the ordinancesand laws of the saidcityof Louisvillenot
inconsistent herewith .
§3 . No second -hand or "junk " store dealer shall, at
any time,or under any circumstances,be allowed to pur-
chase goods , wares , merchandise , materials, or things
whatsoever from a minor .
§4. Every second-hand or "junk" storedealershallkeep
a register, which shallcontain the name of the person or
persons from whom the articleor articlesare purchased ,

426 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
date when received,hisor her residenceorplace ofbusiness,
the amount paid forsaid articleor articles, and a fullde-
scriptionof same . Said registershallbe kept in a plain,
legible,English handwrite ,and shallat alltimes be open
totheinspectionofthe officersofthe law inthedischarge
of their officialduties ; and if sufficientinformation can-
not be gained from an inspectionofsaidregister,itshall,
upon the requestof any officer,be the duty of the pro-
prietor of any such second -hand or "junk " storeto per-
mit and allow said officer or officers to examine and
inspect any and all articlesin said establishment be-
longing to,or temporarily leftin charge of,said proprie-
tor .
§5 . Any second -hand or "junk " store dealer or pro-
prietorwho shallneglect, violate, or refuseto comply
with eitheror allof the provisionsof thisact,shallbe
deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and,upon conviction
thereof,shall be fined not less than twenty dollars nor
more than two hundred dollars for each offense, and in
addition to said fine,his or her licensemay ,at the discre-
tionof a jury,be forfeitedto the use of the cityof Lou-
isville.
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 8, 1878.
CHAPTER 890 .

of Kentucky :

ty,be, and he is hereby,authorizedand empowered to
appoint a deputy ,who shallperform the same duties and

CHAPTER 897 .
AN ACT declaringRed Lick Fork of StationCamp creek,in Estillcounty,
a navigable stream .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Red Lick Fork of Station Camp creek, in
Estill county ,be declared a navigable stream from its
mouth to the mouth of Locust branch .
§ 2. Nothing in thisact shallbe so construed as to pre-
vent owners of land on said creekfrom placing water-
gaps across the same ,provided said water -gaps do not
interfere with navigation .
§3 . This act shall take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 9 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 898 .
AN ACT to authorizethepeopleof Lagrange and Westportvotingpre-
einets,in Oldham county,to vote a tax forthe purpose of buildingturn-
pike roads in said precincts .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The judge of the Oldham county court shallorder
an electionto be held in the Lagrange and Westport vot-
ing precincts, at the usual places of voting in said pre-
cincts, within thirtydays after the passage of this act,
which order shallbe published in a newspaper published
in said county , to ascertain the sense of the people in
said precinctsas to whether they willimpose upon them-
selves a tax for the purpose of constructing turnpike
roads as hereinafter provided . Said election shall be
held at the voting places as aforesaid by the officersof
election lastappointed by the county court for said pre-
cincts respectively. If, in any case, there are not a
sufficientnumber of officersof electionleftin the pre-
cincts aforesaid to hold the election, then the county
court shall appoint others to fillthe places then leftva-
cant . Said election shall be held, and the poll-books
returned to the clerk of the county court, as now pro

444 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vided by law in cases of other elections,except that no
person shallbe allowed to vote outside of his voting pre-
cinct wherein he resides at said election . None other
than the legally qualifiedvoters of said precincts shall
vote,as is provided by law in the case of general elec-
tions . At said election there shall also be chosen in each
precinct(viz: Lagrange and Westport) three persons as
turnpike commissioners , who shall be known as the turn-
pike commissioners of the precinctsby which they are
elected .
§2 . On the fourth day following said election the clerk
of the county court,and the nearest justiceof the peace
of Oldham county livingnearestto the court-house of
said county and the sheriffof the county , who are here-
by constituteda board for that purpose, shallcompare
the poll-books , and shall forthwith certifythe result of
said election, embodying in their certificatethe number
of votes for,and the number of votes against,the ratifi-
cation of this act, and the number of votes cast for turn-
pike commissionersin eachprecinct; and they shallcause
their certificate to be entered of record in the order -book
of the county court.
§3 . If in eitherprecinctthere shallbe a majorityof
the votes cast in favor of the ratificationof this act,then
the same shallbe in force in said precinct from the time
the certificateof election is recorded ; and the turnpike
commissioners elected in said precinct shallbe declared,
by order of the county court, to be turnpike commission-
ersforthe succeeding two years,and untiltheirsuccessors
areelectedand qualified. If ineitherprecinctthereshall
be a majority of the votes cast opposed to the ratification
of thisact,then the same shallnot be in force in said pre-
cinct,nor shallthe commissioners be declared as officers
under thislaw in eitherprecinctwhich may ratifythis
act. Thereshall,on thefirstMonday inAugustofeach
alternateyear thereafter,be electedthree commissioners
to succeed those previouslyelected, untilthisact shall
have been in force in said precinct fiveyears .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 445
§ 4. In eitherprecinctwhich may refuse, at the first
election ordered under this act,to ratifythe same , there
shall,on the firstMonday of August in eachyearthere-
after,for fiveyears,be held an electionfor the same pur-
pose and in the same manner as the firstelection, and
the resultthereof shall be certified, and shalloperate in
like manner ,if at any one of such elections this act be
ratifiedby such precinct. Each one of the commission-
ers elected in eitherprecinct which shall, at any election,
ratifythisact, or at any election for commissioners ,shall
take an oath that he willfaithfullydischarge his duties
under this act,which shall be noted of record in the coun-
ty court order-book , and shall execute bond , payable to
the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,with good surety,in the
sum of fivehundred dollars,conditioned that he willfaith-
tullydischargethe dutiesof his officeas turnpikecom-
missionerof Lagrange or Westport precinctof Oldham
county ,which bond shallbe filedin the clerk'sofficeof
the county court,and may be sued upon by any person
having a rightof action thereon,in the name of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ,to his use. Said commissioners
shallconstitutea board ,and may sue and be sued ,by the
name and styleof the Turnpike Commissionersforeither
Lagrange,or the Westport Precinctof Oldham County ,
accordingto which they respectivelybelong.
§6 . The said commissioners shall,immediately afterthey
shallhave qualifiedunder thisact,recommend by writing,
which shallbe filedin the county court clerk'soffice, one
of theirnumber ,who shallbe thus appointedthe assessor
for his voting precinct, who shall once in each year , for
fiveyearsfrom the time thisact takes effect,in thisvoting
precinct,before the firstday of July,take and return to
the board of turnpike commissioners an assessment of
the number of male persons resident in his voting pre-
cinct over the age of twenty -one years; the number of
acresof land owned by each person livingin hisvoting
precinct lying in the precinct,the value thereof,the num-
ber of town lots and theirvalue, and the value, includ-
ing cash and cash notes,of the personalpropertyof such
person livingin his voting precinct, and of the name and

446 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
residence of each person not residingin his voting pre-
cinct owning land or town lots therein, with the number
of acres and value of such lands or town lots .
§7 . The said commissioners shall,in like manner , rec-
ommend one of theirnumber ,who shallbe thus appointed
collectorof the tax to be imposed by this act,who may
be the same person who holds the officeof assessor, and
who shallexecute bond ,with good surety,in the sum of
five thousand dollars,payable to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , conditioned that he will faithfullydischarge
the duties of his office, and willcollectand pay over all
money coming to his hands , and will turn over allbooks
and money on hand to his successor in office,which bond
shallbe filedin the clerk'sofficeof the county court,and
may be sued on as provided forthe bonds of the commis-
sioners .
§ 8. The said commissioners shall,in likemanner ,recom-
mend one of theirnumber ,who shallbe thus appointed
treasurerof the turnpikefunds of his voting precinct,
who may be the same person who holds the officeof
assessoror collector,or both,and whose dutyitshallbe
to receive of the collectorall money collected by him
under thisact,and pay the same out on the written order
of the board of turnpike commissioners , signed by a ma-
jorityof said board,and entered upon the books of the
board , and who shall execute a similar bond to that of
the collector,which shallbe disposedof and have effect
as the bond of the collector; but if the office of collector
and treasurerbe heldby the same person,the penaltyof
the bond shall be five thousand dollars for both offices, or
five thousand dollars for the office of treasurer .
§ 9. The turnpike commissioners for eithervoting pre-
cinct(viz: Lagrange and Westport precincts) in which
thisact shallever be in force shall, within ten days after
the return of the assessor'sbook to them , in each year ,
assessagainsteach person returnedon the assessor'slist
a tax of fiftycents on each one hundred dollarsin value
of allproperty assessed, and a poll-tax of twenty-five
centsupon each male overtwenty-one years old,resident

in said voting precinct, and shall affixto the name of

sessed against him for said year ; and shall cause to be

stated periods,not more than one month apart,at some
place in theirvoting precincts,of which noticeshallbe

pike commissioners , as to which would be the best mode

panied by a writtenstatementon oath,thatthe services
were rendered .

half of one per cent.on the totalamount of tax assessed,

§ 15. The collector shall be allowed for his services

be attested by one member of the board , who does not

§ 16. The treasurer shall be allowed one half of one
per cent. on allmoneys receivedand accounted forby

from the settlementwith the specialcommissioner,and
allowed and paid on the orderof the board.

CHAPTER 899 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Henry Stanberry, W. C. P. Breckinridge,and
B. F. Buckner .
WHEREAS , Pursuant to the power and authority vested
in the Governor of this Commonwealth by the act of
February 24 , 1873, entitled"An act to protect the inter-
est of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in certain cases ,"
His Excellency,PrestonII.Leslie,did,in the year 1873,
and subsequent to the passage of said act,contract with
and employ Henry Stanberry,William C. Breckinridge,
and B. F. Buckner (the lasttwo named as partners,under
the firm styleof Breckinridge& Buckner), attorneyat
law , to represent and attend to the interestof the Com-
monwealth in certain cases , viz : in four civil actions ,
then depending in the Circuit Court of the United States
for the Districtof Kentucky ,brought to oust the regu-
larlyelected officersof the cityof Lexington ,and install
in their places the defeated candidates,and a number of
criminalprosecutionsagainst the electionand otherofficers
of the cityof Lexington ; said civilactions and criminal
prosecutions having been institutedunder the provisions
of the act of Congress approved 31st day of May , 1870,
sometimes called the enforcement act, but commonly
known as the Ku -Klux act; and whereas ,the Common-
wealth was interestedto defeat the said actions and pros-
ecutions,and thereby to establishthe validityof the State
laws regulatingelections,and the saidStanberry,Breck-

454 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
inridge, and Buckner did,pursuant to the said contract
and employment ,attend to said cases before the United
States Commissioners , the Circuit Court of the United
States for the Districtof Kentucky , and the Supreme
Court of the United States,to which last named court
one of said criminalcaseswas carriedby appeal; and
whereas ,the amount of compensation which said attor-
neys were to receive was not fixedand agreed on by said
contract; therefore,in remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the question as to what is a just and fair
compensation for said servicesof Henry Stanberry , Wm .
C. P. Breckinridge,and B. F. Buckner ,be ,and the same
is hereby , referred to the determination of three sensible
and discreetcitizens,one to be selectedby the Governor ,
one by said Stanberry , Breckinridge, and Buckner , and
the third by the two persons thus selected. Said com-
missioners shall,after firstbeing duly sworn , and after
notifyingthe AttorneyGeneral of the time and place of
theirmeeting ,proceed to consider alllegal evidence that
may be presented to them by said parties, or by the At-
torneyGeneral,and shallrender theirfindingor award
in writing ; and if they disagree,two of said commis-
sioners shallhave power to fix the sum of such reason-
able compensation . Said finding or award shall be filed
with the Auditor of Public Accounts , and shall be pre-
served by him as part of the records of his office,and
shall be conclusive evidence of the value of said ser-
vices : Provided,however, That the award to be made by
said commissioners shallnot exceed the sum of forty-five
hundred dollars .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 9, 1878.

CHAPTER 904 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the Headquartersand Steele'sRun Turnpike
Road Company ,Nicholas county.
WHEREAS , Five continuous miles of the Headquarters
and Steele'sRun Turnpike Road ,in Nicholas county ,Ken-
tucky,have been completed and a toll-gate erected on
said completed part,leaving one and threefourthsof a
mile of said road yet to be completed ; and whereas , the
sixth mile of said road is now in course of construction ,
and will soon be completed , and when completed ,the
three fourths of a mile will be put under construction;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That when said sixth mile shall have been com-
pleted by the company ,and examined and received by
two justicesof the peace and some competent engineer ,
all three of whom to be selected for that purpose by the
county judge of Nicholas county , and who shall report
their examination to the county court,the said company
shall have the right and privilege of erecting a toll-gate
on said road,at or near Hooktown ,at which gate they
may charge and collect tolls at rates not exceeding one
half of the rates now fixed by the General Statutes for
toll-gatesstandingfivemilesapart. Any personevading
or attemptingto evade the payment of tollat saidgate,
shall be liableto the same penaltyas isnow inflictedby
the General Statutes for a like offense.
§ 2. This actto take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 9, 1878.

AN ACT to alter and establish the line between the counties of Scott and
Harrison .

CHAPTER 908 .
AN ACT for the benefit of A. Goldstein .
WHEREAS ,It appears that A. Goldstein did obtain a
license from the county court of Boyle county , in the
month of October, 1875, to peddle and vend spectacles
in the State at large,and for which he paid the sum of
one hundred dollars,the tax required thereforby section
4,chapter84,General Statutes; and whereas,itappears
that at the time said license was obtained , he was a citi-
zen of thisState, and was vending exclusivelyspectacles
the manufacture of this State , and was not therefore sub-
ject to said tax,being exempted from the same by the
provisions of an act to amend said chapter 84 , approved
February 21st, 1874 ; wherefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditorof PublicAccounts isherebydi-
rected to draw hiswarrant upon the Treasurer in favor
of A. Goldstein,for the sum of one hundred dollars, to
be paid out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise
appropriated .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 9 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 909 .
AN ACT for the benefitof John W. Bronaugh , of Jessamine county.
WHEREAS , On the 16th day of October , 1871, Preston
H. Leslie,then the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , issued,or cause to be issued, and published in
the FrankfortYeoman ,and othernewspapers published

462 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in the State of Kentucky , his proclamation offeringa
reward of two hundred dollars,each ,for the apprehen-
sion and convictionof robbers and burglars, who robbed
toll-gates in Jessamine county ,and persons engaged in
shooting on Saturday night,the 30th of September , 1871,
at said gates,and theirdeliveryto the jailerof Jessamine
county; and whereas, one Parker Riley, of color, was
apprehended by John W. Bronaugh ,in the cityof Lex-
ington, Kentucky , and delivered to the authorities of
Jessamine county,on the day of , 1871,and was
afterwards tried and convicted, and sentenced to the
State Penitentiaryfor the period of seven years,and is
now so confined, and no part of said reward having
been paid to said John W. Bronaugh ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant upon the Treasurer
of this Commonwealth , payable to John W. Bronaugh ,
for the sum of two hundred dollars,the amount specified
in the Governor's proclamation and reward , which re-
mains unpaid .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 9 , 1878.

CHAPTER 919 .
AN ACT to authorize the trustees of common school district No. 50 , in
Mason county,to levy and collecta tax forthe erectionof schoolbuild-
ings .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trusteeof common school districtNo. 50,
in Mason county , shall order an election to be held in
said districton the firstSaturday of July, 1878,to take
the sense of the qualifiedwhite voters of said districton
theproprietyof levyinga tax on allthe taxableproperty
in saiddistrictbelongingto white persons,to purchase a
lotof ground ,fence,ornament , and erect thereon a neat ,
commodious , and comfortable school -house , for the sole
use and benefit of the white pupils of said district,the
amount of tax to be fixed by said trustee in his order of
election,which shallbe forty cents on the one hundred
dollars' worth of taxable property in the district; and the
saidamount of fortycents shallbe leviedeach year for
fiveyears on the taxable property in said district; but the
aggregate amount of all the tax levied shall not exceed
two thousand dollars ; and whenever said amount of tax ,
at fortycents on the hundred dollars,shallaggregate the
above named sum ,then thelevyoffortycentsshallcease,
even ifthefiveyearshave notelapsed. The lotofground
and the buildings thereon shallbe deeded to the common
school trustees, and their successors forever .
§2 . The trustee shall appoint two judges and a clerk
to conduct said election,the polls to be opened at nine
o'clock in the forenoon and closed at five o'clock in the
afternoon ; and if a majority of all those voting are in
favor of levying said tax, the trustee shall immediately
enterthesame of recordon hisjournal. All personsen-
titled to vote for trustees shall be legal voters at this elec-
tion. The trusteeshallappoint the marshal of the town of
Chester to collectsaid tax; said marshal shallexecute bond ,
to be approved by said trustee,for a faithfulcollectionof
said taxes,and for failureto perform hisduties,he shall
be responsibleon his bond; said bond to be executed
each year before he collectsany tax. Said marshal shall

468 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
receiveforhiscompensationsuch an amount as the trus-
tee may agree to give,not to exceed fiveper centum upon
the amount collected .
§ 3. Said marshal of Chester shall,on or before the first
day of July, 1878, and at the same time each succeeding
year,so long as saidtax may be to collect,make outfrom
the assessor'sbooks in the county clerk'soffice,returned
forthatyear,a correctlistof allpropertyin saiddistrict
subjectto be taxed under thisact,and may supply any
omissions that may have been made by the assessor, and
to proceed to collectthe tax levied thereon; and as fast
as collectedhe shallpay same over to the commissioners
hereinafternamed , and same shallbe applied by said
commissioners to pay for said lot and buildings.
§4.Allpropertylistedin saiddistrictforStaterevenue
shallbe subjectto taxes under thisact;and no property
subjectto taxationunder thisact shallbe exempt from
distraint or sale for taxes thereon .
§ 5. In case the number of trustees should be increased
by the Legislature,then where the word trusteeisusedin
thisact,thesame shallbe construedas applying to trus-
tees .
§6 . The marshal of Chester,so faras his dutiesunder
this act are concerned ,shall be termed a collector,and
shallhave all the power to collectthe tax that is au-
thorizedby thisact that the sheriffhas in the collec-
tion of the State revenue .
§7 . That the three trusteeselectedby the peopleare
hereby appointedcommissionersto purchase saidlotand
letout saidbuildingand improvements,and in the event
of eitherfailingto act,the others shall fillthe vacancy
by appointment. Said commissionersshallgivebond to
the trustee for the faithfulperformance of their duties,
and shallaccountforallmoneys paid tothem by the col-
lector,or from private giftsor bequests.
§ 8. This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April9,1878.

CHAPTER 924 .
AN ACT to authorize the erection of a school building in colored school dis-
trictNo. 1, in Ohio county, and to levy a tax therefor.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the trustee of colored
school districtNo. 1, Ohio county , and his successors,to
cause to be erected in said district a school -house , not
exceeding in value,including the necessary furniture to
be placed therein, the sum of one thousand dollars,for
the use and benefit of the colored school children in said
district .
§2 . The said building when erected shall not be used
for any purpose,exceptthatof a school-house
§ 3. For the purpose of erecting said house the school
trustee of said district, and his successors , shall have

on allreal and personal estateowned by the colored citi-
zens in said district,of not exceeding one dollaron each
one hundred dollars' worth , and shall have power and
authorityto levy and collectan annual capitationtax on
allcoloredpersons,over twenty-one years of age in said
district, of not exceeding fivedollars.
§ 4. The said trustee, and his successors, shall have
power and authorityto call out for labor on said build-
ing all the male colored citizens in said district between
eighteen and fiftyyears of age ,not exceeding two days
in any one week ; and a failureto work , when called out,
may be prosecutedby saidtrusteeand successorsas road
hands are now prosecuted for failing to labor on roads
and the same fine collected, which shall be for the use
and benefit of said district .
§5 . The tax authorized to be collected herein shall be
collectedas school taxes are by law now authorized to
be collected .
§ 6. This act shall not be in force until ratifiedby a
majority of all colored voters in said districtwho vote
on the ratificationhereof, at an election to be held by
the trustee of said district.
§ 7. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 9, 1878.

476 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 926 .
AN ACT to establishan institutionof learning in the town of Corinth, in
Grant county, Kentucky ,to be known as the Corinth Academy .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an institutionof learning be ,and the same
is hereby , established in the town of Corinth, Grant
county,to be known by the name and styleof the Corinth
Academy .
§2 . That John T. Harris, G. W. Trimnell, W. A. Mil-
lion,James M. Boone , and W. H. Dougherty,and their
successors, are hereby appointed a board of trustees,and
as such shallhave perpetual succession;may sue and be
sued ,contract and be contracted with; and as such may
hold any property, money ,or choses in action, which at
any time or in any manner may belong to said institu-
tion .
§ 3. Said board may make and execute such rules and
regulations for the government of said academy ,not in
violationof law , as they may deem necessary for itsgov-
ernment and welfare .
§ 4. That said board may appoint one of itsmembers
president,and a majorityshallconstitutea quorum to do
business;and when a vacancy or vacancies occur in said
board from any cause,itshallbe the duty of those remain-
ing in officeor the survivorto fillsuch vacancy or vacan-
cies .
§ 5. Said board may appoint a treasurer,who shallexe-
cute bond , with good security, to discharge such dutiesas
may be required of him by said board .
§6 . That the common school of Corinthdistrict,and
part of districtNo. 23 ,in Grant county , which desireto
be attached to said Corinth district,shall be taught in
saidacademy ,subjectto theregulationsofsaidboard and
the common school laws ; and said board of trustees shall
be entitled to receive the common school fund to which
said districtmay be entitled,and shallbe subjectto the
same penaltiesfor neglectof duty and malfeasance in
office,during the term the common school is taught,as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 477
trusteesof common schools are, except removal from
office .
§ 7. Said trusteesmay raise by subscription a capital
stock, not exceeding one thousand dollars, payable in
shares of twenty -five dollars each ,payable at such time
as the board may direct, to purchase or erecta school
building; and when ,in theirjudgment ,a sufficientamount
of money is subscribed,the stockholdersshallmeet and
electof theirnumber fivetrustees,who shallgovern said
building in addition to the duties required of them men-
tioned in this charter; and said stockholders may elect
trusteesannually at such time and manner as they may
deem best, and each share shallbe entitledto one vote in
elections and the transaction of business .
§8. That no principalof said academy shallbe em-
ployed by the trusteesunless he or she shallhave a first-
class certificate from some examiner or examiners of
common school teachers in thisState; and said principal
or assistantsshallbe subject to removal by said board of
trustees for incompetence , neglect of duty , or immoral
conduct , and shall be subject at all times to thorough
examination by said board touchingtheirqualifications,
duties,or conduct
§ 9. That the said corporation is hereby vested with
power to grant diplomas in conformitywith the provis-
ions of itsconstitutionand by -laws . The board of trus-
tees may appoint a board of visitorsfor said academy ,
who shallbe considered an advisory board of officers,
subject to change or removal by the trustees.
§ 10. The common school property now in said district
may be condemned by the common school commissioner
and sold,and the proceeds of saidsaleshallbe used to
purchase desks and furnitureforsaidacademy .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 9, 1878.

CHAPTER 927 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof James M. Tucker , Thomas Allen,Henry Hud-
son , and R. A. Crump , of Warren county .
WHEREAS , James M. Tucker , Thomas Allen,and Henry
Hudson live near the line dividing the counties of War-
ren and Edmonson ,on the Warren side ; and whereas ,
part of theirlands liein the county of Edmonson , and the
present law requiring them to listsaid land in Edmonson
county works a great hardship; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

Henry Hudson be allowed to listalltheir lands for taxa-
tion in the county in which they live, not to exceed three
hundred acres,and pay theirtax in the same county .

ered as being in the county of Edmonson ,and he may
listhis land fortaxationthat may be in Warren county
in the county of Edmonson .

AN ACT to amend an act,entitled" An act toincorporatethe town of Ful-
ton, in Fulton county," approved February 8 , 1872.

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the corporate limitsof said town be divided
into three wards , known as the first, second , and third
wards, bounded as follows: firstward to comprise all
that part of the town east of the MississippiCentral
Railroad ; second ward that part of the town west of
the MississippiCentralRailroadand east of the creek
known as Harris Fork ; the third ward that part of the
town west of the creek before mentioned .
§2 . And be itfurtherenacted, That the votersof each
ward shallelect,at theirregularelection,(2) two council-
men , who shallbe known as the City Council of Fulton,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 479
and theirpowers shallbe the same as vestedin the board
of trusteesin the originaland amended chartersof said
town .
§3 . And be itfurtherenacted, That the police judge of
said town shall preside over any and all meetings of
said council , and be known as chairman of said board .
Approved April 9 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 929 .
AN ACT to pay Hiram T. Warder and the familyof John Ruggles,deceased,
the reward heretoforeofferedforthe arrestof JesseUnderwood ,a fugitive
from justice.
WHEREAS , On the 24th day of January , 1872,Governor
P. II. Leslie,by hisproclamation , offereda reward of five
hundred dollars for the arrest of Jesse Underwood , in-
dicted formurder by the Bath circuitcourt,and who was
then a fugitivefrom justice; and whereas ,Governor Jas.
B. McCreary ,by a general proclamation ,made a revoca-
tion of all rewards offered previous to the 3d day of
May , 1874,which included the reward offered for said
Underwood ; and whereas , Hiram T. Warder , sheriffof
Lewis county , without knowing of the revocation of said
reward, and aided by said John Ruggles, pursued said
Underwood ,who had come into the State,and was again
escaping,and at the riskof hisown life,and at the ex-
pense of the lifeof saidJohn Ruggles,who was shotand
killedby saidUnderwood ,the saidWarder captured and
secured saidUnderwood ,who is now in the custody of
the jailerof Bath county; and whereas, it appears to
this Legislature that the claim of the said Hiram T.
Warder , and the family of the said John Ruggles , de-
ceased , to the amount said reward ,is meritorious and
just; now ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he
is hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant upon the Treas-
urer in favor of Hiram T. Warder for the sum of five
hundred dollars, one half of said money to be for the

480 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
use of the said Warder , and the other half thereof to
the mother of John Ruggles, deceased; and the Treas-
urer is directed to pay the said warrant to the said
Warder , when presented at his officefor that purpose ,
out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated .
§2 . This act to be in forceand have effectfrom itspas-
sage .
Approved April 9, 1878.

circuitcourt,the Commonwealth of Kentucky recovered
judgment against J. N. Culton , late sheriffof Jackson
county,and hissureties,G. S Robinson,T. H. Reynolds,
Jason Garrett,V. W. Morris, Martha J. Culton, and G.
W. Adkins, forthe sum of ($ 1,63179) sixteenhundred
and thirtyone dollarsand seventy-nine cents,being rev-
enue due from the county of Jackson to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky for the year 1872 ; and whereas ,
writs of execution have been issued, from time to time ,
on said judgment , and the same have been levied on the
propertyof said sheriffand sureties,and theirproperty,
both personal and real,has all been sold to satisfysaid
judgment ,and the said sheriffand suretieshave been re-
duced to utterinsolvency,by reason of the sale of their
property forthe payment of said judgment ; and whereas ,
there isa small portionof the principal of said judgment
yet unpaid,alsotheinterestand damages thereon; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

they are hereby,releasedfrom the payment of any and
all interestand damages on said judgment : Provided ,
They make fulland complete payment of the principal
of said judgment by the firstday of January , 1880.

therefore ,

CHAPTER 955 .

WHEREAS , It appears that at several calledterms of the
Marion circuitcourt,held in 1876-'7 ,held by Judge Ows-
ley, special judge under appointment of the Governor ,
forthe purpose of tryingone John C. Northcraft,on an
indictment for the murder of Thomas H. Chandler ,town
marshal of Lebanon ,Richard Wathen rendered services
for the Commonwealth in summoning witnesses and ju-
rors; many of the jurors having to be summoned from
adjoining counties; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he
is hereby , authorized to draw his warrant on the Treas-
urer in favor of Richard Wathen ,for the sum of ninety-
nine dollars .
§ 2. This act to take effect and be in force from and
afterits passage .
Approved April 9 , 1878.

CHAPTER 968 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John Jorden , an idiotof Lawrence county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Kentucky be ,and he ishereby ,
directed to pay to Greenville Lackey ,committee for John
Jorden,an idiotof Lawrence county,Kentucky ,for the
benefitof saididiot,the sum of ($37 50)thirty-seven dol-
lars and fiftycents,the amount of a claim allowed said
idiotat the May term of the Lawrence circuitcourt,1876,
and payment refused by the Auditor , in consequence of
the inquest having expired before that time ; and the in-
quest being had at the next term of the court, and he
again being found to be an idiotin the same condition,
and drawing as such up to thistime .
Approved April 9 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 969 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof R. A. Mitchell, of Montgomery county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Richard A. Mitchell,clerk of the Montgomery
circuitcourt,ishereby authorizedand empowered toprac-
ticehis profession as an attorney at law ,in allcourts of
thisCommonwealth excepttheMontgomery circuitcourt.
§ 2. That thisact shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 9, 1878.
CHAPTER 971 .
AN ACT to repeal an act to amend an act, entitled " An act to increase the
jurisdictionof quarterlycourtsin Hickman and other counties."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the provisions of said amended act,making
itapplicableto Montgomery county ,is hereby repealed.

Approved April 9, 1878.

CHAPTER 974 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Louisa .

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 976 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Knox county to issuebonds to
raisemoney to pay offthe outstandingbonds of said county issuedunder
and by virtueof an act approved January 26, 1871.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Knox county ,a majority
of the justicesof the peace of said county being present

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 537
and concurring therein, at a regular term of said court,
be , and the same is hereby , authorized and empowered
to issuethe bonds of said county , and cause same to be
sold, in any sum not exceeding twelve thousand dollars
in the aggregate ,and bearing an annual rate of interest
not exceeding seven per centum per annum ,to be paid
annually at such time and place in said county as the
said court may direct. The proceeds arising from the
sale of said bonds shallbe used solelyand exclusive for
the purpose of paying off the outstanding bonds of said
county , issued under and by virtue of an act approved
January 26, 1871, entitled" An act to authorize the coun-
ty court of Knox county to issuebonds to raisemoney to
erect a court-house and other public buildings in said
county ;" said bonds shallbe in written or printedform , as
saidcourtmay direct,and shallbe made payable to the
purchaseror hisorder,and shallbe signedby the county
judge of said county , and countersigned by the clerk of
said court, and shall be redeemable at the pleasure of
said court at any time within ten years from the date
thereof; said bonds may be in any denominations that
said court may designate,and shallbe soldfor not less
than their face value .
§2 . The judge of said court shallcause a record to be
made of allthe bonds that may be issued under the pro-
visionsof thisact, showing the amount and date of each
bond , when due , and to whom issued.
§3 . For the purpose of paying off said bonds ,princi-
pal and interest, said court, composed of the county
judge and a majorityof the justicesof the peace in said
county ,may increase the county levy, not exceeding one
dollarand twenty-fivecents ($ 1 25) on each person in
saidcounty liablefor poll-tax,over and above what is
necessary for ordinary county purposes ,and may levy an
ad valorem tax not exceeding twenty cents on each one
hundred dollars' worth of propertyin the county subject
to taxation for State revenue ; and the sheriff shall col-
lect and account for same in the same manner as the
county levy and revenue tax are now collected and ac-
counted for by law , having the same right to distrain

538 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
therefor; and he and his sureties shall be liable therefor
in likemanner as they are liableforcounty levy and rev-
enue .
§4 . The county court of said county may appoint a
commissionerto collectfrom the sheriffthe money raised
by thisact and pay out the same ,who shallbe required
firstto execute bond ,with good and sufficientsureties,for
the faithfulperformance of his duty as such commission-
er; said bond shallbe executed to the county court of
Knox county , on which suitmay be in any court having
jurisdictionin the name of said court.
§ 5. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 9 , 1878.

CHAPTER 979 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Parksville , in Boyle county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the civiljurisdictionof the police judge of
the town of Parksville be increased to,and be the same as,
that of justicesof the peace of thisCommonwealth .
§2 . That afterthe expiration of the term of office of
the present incumbents ,the policejudge and marshal of
said town shallhold theirrespectiveofficesforthe period
of two years instead of one ,as now provided by law , and
untiltheirsuccessors are duly qualified.
§ 3. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 980 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof schooldistrictNo. 23,Lyon county .
WHEREAS ,In July,1877,in districtNo. 23,Lyon county,
a tax of twenty -five on every one hundred dollars'
worth of property was voted to build a much -needed
school -house , and the trustee of said districtconstructed
and had built a school-house in the place of one pre-
viously legally condemned ; and whereas , after said ex-
pense had been incurred, certain tax-payers refused to
pay said tax, whereby the trustee and workmen are
deprived of the money justlydue them ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the trustee of districtNo. 23 , Lyon county ,
is hereby empowered to collectsaid tax, with power to
distrainand pay thejustindebtednessdue forthe build-
ing of said school -house .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10 , 1878.

eral Statutes .

ment ,garnishment ,distressforrent, or fee-bills; also one
sewing machine ,the property of any female who works

partsof said act to which thisisan amendment ,and all

or in conflictwith the provisionsof this act, are hereby
repealed .
§2 . Provided, That the provisionsof thisact shallonly
apply to the cityof Louisville,cityof Covington, and
cityof Newport .

after its passage .

finefrom the said Laughland ,and afterthe remittercame
tosaidcounty he could not collectsame ; now ,therefore,

CHAPTER 995 .
AN ACT to establisha sinking fund forWashington county , and prescribe
the duties of the commissioners thereof .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Washington county court shall,at itsnext
levyingcourt,electthreepersons,residentsof saidcounty,
commissioners of the sinking fund for said county ,who
shallhold theirofficefor three years,and untiltheirsuc-
cessors are elected and qualified. Before they act,they
shalltake the oath of officeprescribedby the Constitu-
tion and laws of this State : Provided , That at the first
election the commissioners shall cast lots as to who shall
serve one , who two and three years,and each year there-
after one commissioner shall be elected at such annual
meeting of the levying court.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 567
2. That said commissioners shall elect one of their
mumber treasurer of said commissioners ,who ,before he
acts as such , shall execute bond ,with good security, to
be approved by the judge of saidcourt,to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , conditioned that he will faithfully
and promptly collectallmoneys due saidboard,and pay
the same over to the persons entitledto it,and perform all
acts requiredof him by the said board, or the county
court,or thatmay be imposed on him by law, on which
any person aggrieved may maintain an action; and one
recovery shallnot bar another so long as said bond has
been violated .
§3 . It shall be the duty of said commissioners to de-
mand and receive allmoneys that may be by law appro-
priated to pay the principal and interestof allbonds or
other indebtedness created to pay the debt or liabilityof
Washington county,growing out of her subscriptionin
aid of the Cumberland and Ohio RailroadCompany ,and
to cause the same to be paid out on said liability, first
paying the intereston bonds,then the bonds as they fall
due : Provided, That if, at any term before any of said
bonds falldue ,there shallbe any surplus money on hand ,
afterpaying the interest,the commissioners may purchase
for the county any of said bonds ,but shall not pay for
them anything over par; and if said bonds cannot be
bought at par or less,then they may invest said surplus in
other good bonds ,bearing interest at six per cent .,and
hold them forthe use of the county ,untilthe bonds of the
county can be bought as herein provided ; then he may
sellsaid bonds ,and invest the proceeds in county bonds .
§ 4. The said treasurer or the commissioners shall settle
with the sheriffof Washington county at the time he is
required to settlehis accounts for county levy, and re-
ceive from him the amount he may be found owing , and
shall reportthe same to the levying court for itsapproval
or correction;and when approved or corrected,the treas-
urer may ,in the name of " The Commissioners of the
Sinking Fund for Washington ," recover the same by suit,
and tenper cent.damages forthe failureto pay the same

568 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
over, besidesinterestfrom the day the settlementwas
made .
§5. The saidcommissionersshall,at each county levy
court,reporttheiractsto said courtfor theirapproval.
They shallkeep a record of theirdoings,which shallbe at
alltimes open to the inspection of the county court judge
and the said levying court. The county court shall,at
each levying court,levy a tax of not exceeding fiftycents
on each hundred dollarsof the property liableto taxation
for revenue purposes in said county ,which shallbe first
applied to pay the intereston said railroaddebt, and the
balance as prescribedin sectionthree of this act,which
shallbe collectedby the sheriffand paid over when the
law requireshim to pay the county levy: Provided,That
said commissioners shallhave power to move said court
for better security on the sheriff'sbond , and the court
shallhave power to requirehim to give such surety, and
in default,to declare the officevacant ,and appoint a col-
lectorof said tax, who shalltake the oaths and give the
bond requiredby law of the sherifffor the collectionof
county taxes .
§6 . When the said commissioners shallbuy any of the
said bonds , he shall burn the same in the presence of
the county judge and clerkof the county court,firstmak-
ing a listof the same by number , date,when due , and
amount ,and once in each year burn the coupons forin-
terestin the presence of said officers; and whenever they
pay offany of saidbonds,writeacrossthe face of them
canceled .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878 .
CHAPTER 996 .
AN ACT for the benefit of W. S. Hawkins .
WHEREAS ,It appears that the Governor offered a re-
ward of five hundred dollars for the arrest and conviction
of the murderer of Carrie Anderson ,at Worthville,Car-
rollcounty,Kentucky,on the night of the 17th of July,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 569
1877, and W. S. Hawkins ,in his endeavors to bring the
perpetratorsof said crime to justice,expended large sums
of money for detectives and attorney'sfees,and one of
the parties arrested by him confessed to the crime and
was not tried,but used as a witness for the Commonwealth
in prosecuting the other partieswho were arrestedby him :
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That to reimburse said W. S. Hawkins for his ex-
penses, and to pay him for his labor and effortsin ferret-
ing out said crime ,there ishereby appropriated to W. S.
Hawkins , out of any money in the Treasury not other-
wise expended ,the sum of three hundred and fiftydollars,
and the Auditor of Public Accounts ishereby directed to
draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of said Haw-
kins for said amount .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878 .
CHAPTER 999 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Spencer county .
WHEREAS , By an act passed January 26 ,1865 , authority
was conferred upon the county court of Spencer , a ma-
jority of the justices concurring , to sell the Spencer
County Seminary ,and the lot on which it was situated
in the town of Taylorsville, and to loan out the pro-
ceeds, & c., all of which has been done ; and whereas ,
said sale and conveyance was a diversion of the fund
from the originalinvestment in the Spencer County Sem-
inary under the act approved February 12, 1840 ; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John E. Young ,Wm . P. Beard ,Chas . Hough ,
Ashton P. Harcourt ,and Thos .J. Barker ,who are hereby
constituted trusteesfor that purpose , be , and they are
hereby , authorized and empowered to collect the said
seminary fund now in the hands of a commissioner or

570 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
commissioners appointed by the Spencer county court,and
reinvestthe same in eitherthe originalseminary building,
or in erection of an addition thereto,or other buildings
contiguous,forthe purpose of establishingor maintaining
a high school in the town of Taylorsville,Kentucky ,such
as shallafford,in the usual and accepted sense,facili-
ties of obtaining a finished education by the youth of
said county ; and that said county shall have a lien for
the amount invested or the buildings erected on property
purchased .
§2 . That allacts and parts of acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
§3 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1000 .

moneys from the town treasuryto Washington county .

of Kentucky :

passage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1001 .
AN ACT amending the charterof the town of Franklin,Simpson county,
and amendments thereto.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section twenty -five of the charter of the
town of Franklin,Simpson county,be,and the same is,
amended so as to authorize the board of trustees of said
town to tax and license all fire or life insurance com-
panieshereafterdoing or proposingtodo businessin said
town . The board is also authorized to tax and license
allvendors of pianos and organs,whether soldby sample
or otherwise, as well as allvendors of buggies ,carriages,
and other pleasurable vehiclesmanufactured out of this
State, same sellingby sample or otherwise.
§2 . That sectiontwenty -seven of said charter be , and
the same is, amended so as to confer upon the collector
of said town all the powers and authoritiesconferred
upon sheriffsin the collectionof taxes; the said collector
being hereby empowered and authorizedto levy upon
and sellany and allkinds of real estatefor the collection

in similar sales .

ordinances of said town ; and said attorney shall be paid
out of the town or citylevy a reasonable salary for his
services,to be fixedby the said board of trustees.

and section eighty-five of the by -laws thereof,be ,and
they are,so amended as that the circuitcourt of Simpson
county shall hereafter have concurrent jurisdictionwith
the board of trusteesand policecourt of saidtown ,in
prohibitingand punishing the offensesmentioned in said
sections,and the circuitcourt may punish such offenses
as isprovided by the general law .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 10,1878.

CHAPTER 1008 .
AN ACT to authorize Amanda Pile to be received into and maintained in
the Central or Western Lunatic Asylum , without cost to her husband ,
Isaac Pile .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

the Western or Central Lunatic Asylum either,is author-

CHAPTER 1009 .
AN ACT to preventhogs from running at largealong the lineof the Dutch
Ridge and Germantown and BridgevilleTurnpike Road , in Bracken
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to let
theirhog or hogs run at large along the line of the Dutch
Ridge and Germantown and BridgevilleTurnpike Roads ,
in Bracken county .
§2 . That ifany person shallpermit theirhogs to run
at large along the line of said roads,he ,she,or they shall
be liable to a fine of not less than two nor more than ten
dollarsfor each and every offense,and costs,to be recov-
eredbeforeany courthaving jurisdiction. The warrant
shallbe issued in the name of the president and directors
of said Dutch Ridge or Germantown and Bridgeville
Turnpike Road Company ,as the case may be; and all
fines collected under this act shall be for the use of said
companies .
§3 . This act shall take effect from and after the first
day of June , 1878.
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1013 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Covington,Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealt
of Kentucky :
§1 . The citycounciland schoolboard of the cityof
Covington shall,each of them ,have power to designate
a depository or depositorieswhere the money ,securities,
or otherpropertybelongingto them ,respectively,shallbe
deposited,and shall, each of them ,have power to declare
how such money , securities, or other propertyshallbe
kept, and in what manner paid out or withdrawn .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 581
§2 . The auditorof the cityshallalsobe the auditorof
the school board . All claims of the cityor school board
shallbe audited and countersigned by the auditor before
the same are placed in the hands of any agent or officer
of the cityor school board for collection. All orders on
the treasurer,or on designated depository of the city or
school board ,shall, in like manner , be audited and coun-
tersignedby the auditor. All contracts made by the city
or school board , and allevidences of indebtedness issued
by either, shall be countersigned by the auditor; and he
shallperform such other duties as may be, from time
to time,prescribed for him by the citycouncil or school
board .
§3 . The auditor shall hold his officefor the term of two
(2) years, and until his successor shallhave been elected
and qualified.
§4 . The auditor,before entering upon the duties of his
office, shalltake the same oath prescribed for the treas-
urer, and shallexecute bond ,in such sum as council may
prescribeby ordinance, for the faithfulperformance of
his duties,to be approved by the citycouncil.
§5 . No member of the city council or school board
shall be accepted on any officialbond , or on any bond
payable to the cityor school board.
§6. The mode of trialand allproceedingsunder this
act may be prescribedby the citycouncil: Provided, That
a vote of not less than two thirds of all the members
of the city council shall be necessary to suspend or re-
move under the proceedingshereinprovidedfor.
§7 . Any member of the citycouncil of Covington ,who
is absent from itsregular meetings for one month , with-
out leave or such excuse as shall be deemed sufficient
by said citycouncil,shall forfeithis seat thereby, and it
shallbe declaredvacant, and the vacancy filledin the
manner providedforin cases of vacancy by removal.
§8. The citycouncilshallhave power to suspend in
all cases where it has the power to remove ,and by the
same vote. The officemade vacant shall be filledby a
pro tem, officerduring the suspension.

582 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 9. All books,documents,and papers relatingto the
affairsor businessof the cityof Covington,which are or
may be filedin the officeof the cityclerk,auditor,treas-
urer,citycivilengineer,or school board ,shallbe deemed
publicrecords; and any personwho shallknowingly de-
stroy,mutilate, or alter,or who shall make false entries
upon the same ,or any of them ,shallbe deemed guilty
of felony,and,upon indictmentby a grand juryand con-
victionbeforeany court of competent jurisdiction,shall
be sentencedto imprisonmentin the penitentiaryfornot
lessthan one year nor more than ten years.
§.10. Any officerof the city of Covington ,or of the
school board of said city, having charge of or being
charged with the custody or collectionof money or val-
uables belonging to the cityor to the school board , who
shallloan,appropriate to hisown use,or refuseto deposit
the same inthe designateddepositoryof the cityof Cov-
ington or of the school board ,as the case may be ,with
intent to cheat or defraud,shall be deemed guilty of em-
bezzlement, and, upon convictionbefore any court of
competent jurisdiction, shall be sentenced to imprison-
ment in the penitentiaryfor a period of not lessthan one
year nor more than ten years for each offense.
§ 11. Any member of the city council of Covington ,
or any member of the school board , who shall receive
money , or other valuable consideration, as an induce-
ment to influence his vote for or against any measure or
proposition before council or before the school board , or
that may be brought before them , shall be deemed guilty
of felony, and ,upon indictment by a grand jury and con-
victionbeforeany courtofcompetent jurisdiction,shallbe
sentenced to imprisonment in the penitentiaryfornot less
than one year nor more than three years .
§ 12. Any person delivering,or offeringto deliver,or
causing to be delivered,to any councilman or member of
the schoolboard of the city,any money or othervaluable
consideration, for the purpose of directlyor indirectly
affectingthe action or vote of any member of the city
council or school board , for or against any matter or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 583
measure that may be brought before eitherof them , shall
be deemed guiltyof felony,and,upon indictmentby a
grand jury and convictionbeforeany courtof competent
jurisdiction,shallbe sentenced to imprisonment in the
penitentiaryfor not lessthan one year nor more than
three years .
§ 13. That the citycouncil shallhave power to assess,
levy, and collect,in the same manner that other taxes of
the cityare assessed,levied,and collected,upon allthe
property taxable in said cityfor general revenue , a tax
not exceeding ten cents on each one hundred dollars' val-
uation,which tax may continue five years,commencing
with the year 1878, which tax said citycouncil shalluse
exclusivelyfor the purpose of procuring the necessary
ground and buildingsfor a jailand work -house ,house of
refuge and correction,or poor -house ,or either of them .
Said specialtax shallnot be appropriatedor appliedto
any other use or purpose : Provided, That so much there-
of,if any , as may remain of the fund raised therefrom ,
if any , unexpended forthe purposes above named , after
January 1, 1884,shallbe transferredinto the sinking fund
and used only in the redemption of the bonds of the city
of Covington . To enable the city council to carry out
the provisions of this section,the cityshall have power
to condemn lands and material for the purposes named ,
which shallbe done as provided in section4, article6 , of
an act passed March 2, 1850, entitled" An act to amend
and reduce into one the several acts concerning the city
of Covington ."
§ 14. The city council may elect an overseer of the
poor ; he shallhold his officeat the pleasure of that body ;
hisdutiesand compensationshallbe prescribedby ordi-
nance ; he shallgive bond ,payable to the citycouncil, in
such sum and with such conditions as may be prescribed
by the citycouncil.
§ 15. That afterthe firstday of January , 1880,the tax-
bills,for alltaxes and titheslevied by the citycouncil for
the public schools in the cityof Covington ,shallbe made
outby thecityclerkof saidcity,and includedin the tax-
billscontainingthe ordinarylevy of said city,and shall

584 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be collectedwith same ,by the same officers,and in the
same manner , that the ordinaryleviesare collectedby
the collectingofficersof said city; and the powers and
duties conferred and required of officersin collectingthe
ordinary citytaxes are hereby conferred and required of
them in collectingthe taxes and tithesleviedforsaid pub-
licschools; and such collectingofficerand his securities
shall be liable, under his officialbond , for any failureto
perform the dutiesof the collectingofficerof the city,
upon which bond suitmay be brought for the use of the
school board, and recovery had for such amount as shall
be found due thereon. All of such sums of money ,when
collectedand paid into the citytreasury,shallbe setapart
to and passed over to the common school tund , and sub-
ject to and drawn out only by the order of the school
board, as provided by law and the ordinancesof the
school board then existing.
§ 16. The citycouncilof saidcityshallhave fullpower
to directand control allassessments in said city,and may
employ any person or attorney, at a fixed salary or fee,
to assistor conduct the assessment or collectingof taxes
in said city.
§ 17. The said citycouncil shallalso appoint one judi-
cious householder in each ward in said city, not of their
own number , who shallact as an equalization board ,in
such room as may be provided by said councilin said
city, and as soon as practicableaftereach regular assess-
ment ,and equalizeas near as may be allassessmentson
propertyfor revenue purposes in said city. Notice of
saidmeetingshallbepublishedby orderof saidcouncil
in not less than ,two newspapers of general circulation
in said city, and not lessthan one week previous to said
meeting .
§ 18. Allpersonsowning propertyin saidcitymay ap-
pear inperson or by agent beforesaidboard of equaliza-
tion and ask for any correction of the assessment made
upon any propertyin said city. The said citycouncil
shall fix the length of time that said board shall be in
session, and the compensation of same ,which shall not
exceed at the rate of three dollars per day , of not less

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 585
than six hours each day for each member , during the
time he shall actuallyserve. A majority of said mem-
bers shallform a quorum for the transaction of theirdu-
ties .
§19 . The cityclerk shallserve as clerk of said board ,
and shallkeep the time of the attendance of each mem-
ber, and certifythe same to the city council,who shall
make the necessary appropriation to pay the same .
§20 . If from any cause any person may not have ap-
peared before said board of equalization, or may feel
aggrieved by the acts of said board,he shallhave the
rightto appear in person or by agent and lay the facts
before said council , and ask to have his assessments or
taxes corrected .
§ 21. Hereafter itshallbe the duty of every person and
company , private or corporate, the holder or owner of
any propertyof any descriptionin said city,legallylia-
ble to be assessed for taxes therein, and from any cause
has escaped being assessed, to listthe same on or before
the firstday of April in each year , with the assessor or
some other officerof said citylegally authorized to take
saidlist; and should any such person failto so listsaid
property,itshallbe the duty of saidcitycouncilto have
the same listedand assessedas other property was listed
and assessedin the year or years thatsaid property es-
caped being listedand assessed, and said council shall
add theretosuch penalty,not exceedingfifteenper cent.
per annum ,as it may judge proper ,for such number of
years as said property has escaped legal assessment .
§ 22. The citycouncil may hereafterdirect that a fair
abstract of the proceedings of the city council, and of
the ordinances by them passed ,shallbe published at the
expense of saidcity, insteadof allof theirproceedings.
Said abstractshallbe certifiedby the president and clerk
of said council, and published,within three days after the
passage thereof,in some newspaper of general circula-
tion in said city.
§23 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 9, 1878 .

of Kentucky :

on the dividing ridge between the water of Salt Lick
and Clark's Fork .

sage .
Approved April 9 , 1878.
