CHAPTER 1018 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An acttoincorporatetheMt.Sterling
Coal Road Company ."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the words " two thirds" of the qualifiedvoters
of said county ,in sectiontwo of the act enabling Menifee

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 591
county to vote a subscriptionto the capital stock of the
Mt. SterlingCoal Road Company be,and the same are
hereby,struckout,and the words " majority" of the qual-
ifiedvoters of said county insertedin theirstead.
§2 . That any precinctor precincts, cityor town , in
the county of Menifee, may vote a subscriptionto the
capitalstock of the Mt. SterlingCoal Road Company ,
application having firstbeen made to the county court
(as now provided for said county), or to the councilmen
or trustees of the cityor town , under the like rules and
restrictionsprovided for said county ; and the sense of
the qualifiedvoters of such precinct, city, or town be
taken thereon ,under all the restrictions,conditions, and
safeguards,conditioned in the act to which thisis amend-
atory. If a majorityof the qualifiedvotersso voting at
saidelection,of any precinctorprecincts,votea subscrip-
tion to the capitalstock of said road, then the county
clerk shallmake the amount of the subscriptionso voted .
If a majority of the votes cast at such election of the
cityor town vote for a subscription,then the mayor ,or
other presiding officer, shall make the subscription so
voted, upon the conditionsupon which it was voted.
The amount to be subscribedin any one or more pre-
cincts,cityor town ,shallbe in the same proportionto
the whole amount to be subscribedas the taxableprop-
erty in the precinct or precincts, city or town ,bears to
the taxable property of the whole county .
§3 . The provisionsof the actto which thisisamenda-
toryshallapplyto the countiesof Morgan and Powell.
The amount to be voted and subscribed by the county of
Morgan shall not exceed one hundred thousand dollars,
and by the county of Powell thirtythousand dollars.
That the applicationforsuch subscriptionshallbe made
to the countiesof Morgan and Powell withinfiveyears
from the passage of thisamendment .
§4. That where any lands or rightsof way may be
requiredby theMt. SterlingCoal Road Company forthe
purposeof constructingtheirroad,and ,forwant ofagree-
ment as to the value thereof,or any other cause,the same
cannot be purchased from the owner or owners ,the same

592 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
may be taken at a valuation,to be made by fivecommis .
sioners, or a majority of them , to be appointed by the
circuitcourt or county court of the county where some
part of the land or rightof way issituated; and the said
commissioners,before they act,shallseverallytake an
oath,beforesome justiceof the peace,faithfullyand im-
partiallyto dischargetheduty assignedthem in making
the said valuation . The commissioners shall take into
considerationthe lossor damage which may occur to the
owner or owners of the land in consequence of the land
being taken or the right of way surrendered, and also
the benefit and advantage he , she, or they may receive
from the erection or establishment of the railroad works ,
and shallparticularlyconsider the nature and amount of
each , and the excess of loss and damage over and above
the benefit and advantage ; shallform the measure and
valuation of said land or right of way ; but the owners
shallbe paid the value of the land . The proceedings of
said commissioners,accompanied with a fulldescription
of the said land or rightof way ,shall be returned, under
the hands and seals of a majority of said commissioners ,
to the court from which the commission is issued,there
to remain a matter of record in case eitherparty to the
proceedingsshallappeal from the valuationto the next
session of the court granting the commission , and give
reasonablenoticeto the oppositeparty of such appeal.
The court shall order a new valuation to be made by a
jury,who shall be charged therewith in the same term,
or as soon as practicable,and theirverdictshall be final
and conclusivebetween the parties,unless a new trial
shallbe granted; and the lands or rightof way so valued
by the commissioners or jury shallvest in the said com-
pany infee-simpleso soon as the valuationmay be paid,
orwhen refused,may be tendered,and the routemay be
condemned to the extent of one hundred and fiftyfeet
wide . When there may be an appeal as aforesaid from
thevaluationof thecommissionersofeitheroftheparties,
the same shallnot prevent the works intending to be con-
structedfrom proceeding; but when the appeal is by the
company requiringthe surrender,they shallbe at liberty

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 593
to proceed with theirworks only on condition of giving
to the opposite party a bond ,with good security,to be
approved by the clerk of the court,where the valuation
is returned,in a penalty equal to double the said valua-
tion and interest, in case the same be sustained ; and in
case it be reversed, for the payment of the valuation
thereafterto be made by the jury and confirmed by the
court: Provided, That when the land cannot be had by
giftor purchase,the operationof the works are not to
be hindered or delayed during the pending of any pro-
ceeding to assess its value as aforesaid,nor shall any
injunction or supersedeas be awarded by any judge or
court to delay the progress of said work : Provided, That
nothing in this act shall authorize any county herein
named to issue bonds .
§5. This act totakeeffectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 9, 1878.
CHAPTER 1019 .
AN ACT to create and establishan additionalmagistrates'and voting dis-
trictin the county of Lawrence .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby created and established an
additional magistrates' and voting districtin the county
of Lawrence ,No. 8 ,called Lyon district,bounded as fol-
lows , to-wit : beginning on top of dividing ridge between
Upper and Lower Laurel creeks on the Johnson county
line; thence with the top of said ridge to Blaine creek ;
thence tothe Elliottcounty line,having the waters of the
Knob branch and itstributariesin the Big Blaine district;
thence with the Elliottcounty line to where it intersects
with the Morgan county line; thence with Morgan county
line where it intersectswith the Johnson county line;
thence with the Johnson county line to the beginning .
The place of voting in said districtshall be at or near
the Forks of Blaine , near Wm . Holbrook's . There shall
be two justicesof the peace and one constable elected
VOL . II- LOC . L.-38

594 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in said districtat the next August election, and at the
regular electionsforsaid officersin each year thereafter,
and they shallbe qualifiedas now provided by law .
§2 . And thisact shallbe in effectfrom and after its
passage .
Approved April 10,1878,
CHAPTER 1020 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof Margaret Garner ,a pauper idiotof Russellcounty.
WHEREAS ,It appears from the recordsin the Auditor's
officethat the inquest in the case of Margaret Garner , a
pauper idiotof Russellcounty, expired June 13, 1875,
and was not again renewed until the May term , 1876 , of
the Russell circuitcourt, thus creating an interregnum
between said dates of eleven months for which she has
drawn no pay ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be authorized
and instructed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in
favor of C. C. Buster,committee of Margaret Garner ,a
pauper idiotof Russellcounty,forsixty-eightdollarsand
seventy-fivecents($6875 ),the amount due her in con-
sequence of the interregnum between the inquests set
forthin the foregoing preamble .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1021 .
AN ACT for the benefitof F. B. Huston ,latesheriffof Union county .
WHEREAS ,It appearsfrom satisfactoryevidencethatF.
B. Huston ,late sheriffof Union county ,acting under the
orders of the Auditor of Public Accounts ,did listfor tax-
ationcertaincoal propertyof Heath ,Bush,and Lyle,in
and around Uniontown ,in saidcounty,fortheyears 1866
to 1875 ,inclusive,and at the valuation of $ 10,490for each
year,said assessmentbeing made in 1875,and because

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 595
of the failure of the owners to list the same for taxation
for eitherof the said previous years; and whereas, it
appears from satisfactoryevidence that said Huston ,in
his settlement with the Auditor , accounted for the taxes
due on said property for said years at that valuation,
and that the county court of Union county , on the first
day of July, 1876,did,by an order of record, reduce the
valuationof said propertyfor the purposes of taxation
duringthe saidyearsfrom the sum of $ 10,490tothe sum
of $ 5,450,and the proprietorsonly paid the saidsheriff
Huston the taxes on said property for said years at the
lattervaluation,thereby entailing a loss upon the said
sheriffof $ 146 56 as paid into the State Treasury for
taxes on said property more than he received;therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be ,and he is hereby , authorized
to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of said
Huston , and in settlement of the said claim , for the sum
of $ 146 56 ,with its legal interestfrom the firstday of
January , 1876 .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1022 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of Elizabethtown ,and to authorize the board
of trusteesof Elizabethtown to issuebonds and fund the presentbonded
debt of said town .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of trustees of Elizabethtown is
hereby authorized to issue the bonds of said town to an
amount in the aggregate not exceeding fiftythousand
dollars, in denominations of not less than five dollars
nor more than one hundred dollars, with coupons at-
tached, the bonds to bear interest at the rate of eight
per cent. per annum in legalcurrency,negotiableand
payable tobearer at Elizabethtown,at the officeof the
treasurerof said town ,at not more than ten years from

596 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
theirdate, the interestto be paid semi-annually. Said
bonds shall he signed by the president of the board of
trustees and countersigned by the clerk of the board of
trusteesof said town ,and the coupons to be signed by
the clerk alone .
§2 . When said bonds are issued,they may be soldby
said trustees; and when sold,itshallbe the duty of said
trusteesto apply the money arisingfrom such sale in re-
deeming the bonds heretoforeissued by said town to the
Elizabethtown and Paducah Railroad Company : Provided,
Said old bonds can be purchased at a sum not exceeding
fortyper cent.of theirfacevalue and fortyper cent.on
their accrued interest: And provided further, The bonds
authorized by thisact shallnot be sold for lessthan their
face value .
§3 . Before the board of trustees shall be authorized to
issue any of the bonds provided forin the foregoing sec-
tion, itshallbe the duty of said trusteesto cause an elec-
tion to be held in said town , to which shall be submitted
to the qualifiedvotersresidingwithin the boundary of
said town , as it existedwhen the citizensof said town
voted to subscribe seventy-five thousand dollars to the
capital stock of said railroad company , the following
question, to-wit : "Are you willing for the trustees of ,
Elizabethtown to issue fittythousand dollars in bonds ,to
be used in retiringthe old bonds of said town , or not ?"
And if a majority of those voting at said election shall
vote in the affirmative,then it shallbe the duty of said
trustees to issue said bonds ; but if a majority of those
voting at said election shall vote in the negative, said
bonds shall not be issued . Said election shall be held
and conducted by the same officers,and in the mode and
manner now authorized for election of trustees of said
town ,except the electionauthorized under thisact shall
be held at such time before the firstday of August , 1878,
as the trusteesshalldirect. Notice of the time and pur-
pose of the electionshallbe given by said trustees,by
publicationin one paper in saidtown ,and not lessthan
ten handbillsposted in as many publicplaces in said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 597
town , at least fifteendays before the day fixed for the
election .
§4 . When the bonds are issued and sold as provided
for in thisact,itshallbe the duty of said trusteesto levy,
and cause to be collected, a tax of one dollar on each
one hundred dollars on allthe real estate and personal
property within the boundary of said town as itexisted
when said subscription of seventy-five thousand dollars
was voted to the Elizabethtown and Paducah Railroad
Company , subjectto taxation under the revenue laws of
this Commonwealth , including the amounts owned by
residents of said town and boundary which ought to be
listedunder the equalization laws of this State. The
sum so raised shallbe applied to paying the semi -annual
interest on said bonds and cost of collection of said tax ,
the surplus is to be applied in retiringthe bonds issued
under this act .
§5 . The bonds issued under this act shall be retired at
the option of the board of trusteesof said town .
§6 . It shallbe the duty of said board of trusteesannu-
ally to appoint an assessorof said town ,the firstappoint-
ment to be made thesecondMonday inApril,1878,and
thesecond Monday in Aprilthereafterin each succeeding
year,whose duty itshallbe,within thirtydays afterhis
appointment and qualification, to make and return to
said board of trusteesan assessment of all the property
subject to taxation in said town and boundary aforesaid
under the revenue laws of this Commonwealth ; and for
his services shallreceive a reasonable compensation , to
be fixedby the said trustees: Provided,That said assessor,
before entering upon the discharge of his office, shall
take an oath that he will faithfullyand impartiallydis-
charge his duties as assessor. Said assessor shallhold
his officefor one year,and untilhis successorshallbe
appointed and qualified.
§7 . Any person owning property in said town subject
to thistax,who shallwillfullyfailor refuseto give in his
or her listof taxable property when called on by the as-
sessor provided for in thisact,shallbe subjected to the

598 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
same painsand penaltiesnow imposedby law forfail-
ing to listproperty for taxationfor revenue purposes
under the general laws of the Commonwealth .
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of the trusteesto appoint a
collector of the said tax of said town ; and the said col-
lector is authorized, for the purpose of collecting said
tax, to distrainreal and personal property, and sell
the same as the sheriffsare authorized by the General
Statutes for the collection of State revenue ; but before
he proceeds to collectthe same ,he shall execute a bond ,
with good security,to be approved by the board of trus-
tees,to the effectthat he will faithfullycollectand pay
over to the treasurerof said town all moneys collected
by him under the provisionsof this act, at such time as
said board of trusteesmay direct- the form of the bond
to be prescribedby the board of trustees: Provided, That
the tax -books shall remain in the hands of the Treasurer
of the board of trusteesfor ninety days afterthe levy is
made , during which time payment of the tax may be
made to him , and afterwhich time they shall be put in
the hands of the tax collector, and fiveper cent.shallbe
added to all taxes so put in the hands of the collector,
payable by the delinquent tax-payers : And provided fur-
ther, That the coupons on the bonds shallbe receivable
for taxes .
§ 9. It shallbe the duty of the clerkof the board of
trustees, within ten days afterthe assessment isreturned,
as providedforin the sixthsectionof thisact,to furnish
said treasurerwith a listof the names of the tax-payers,
and amount of taxablepropertyin saidtown and bound-
ary, as shown by said assessment .
§10 Itshallalsobe the duty of said board to appoint
a treasurer,the appointment to be made annually,imme-
diatelyafterorganization of said board of trustees, whose
duty itshallbe to receiveand pay out upon the orderof
the board of trusteesallmoney that may come to his
hands as treasurer . But before said treasurer shall enter
on the discharge of his duties, he shalltake an oath to
faithfullydischarge his duties,and shallalso execute a
good and sufficientbond forthe faithfuldischargeof his

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 599
dutiesas treasurer,and thathe willfaithfullypay out all
money that may come to his hands when ordered by said
board of trustees,which bond must be approved by the
board of trustees . Said treasurer shall hold his office for
one year from his appointment , and untilhis successor is
appointed and qualified.
§ 11.It appearing that there is now a vacancy in the
officeof trusteesof said town ,it shallbe the duty of the
clerk of the Hardin county court to hold a specialelection
to fillsuch vacancy;saidspecialelectionshallbe held on
the Saturdaybeforethe firstMonday in April,1878,inthe
mode ,manner ,and place now designated by the charter of
said town ; allsubsequent electionsare to be held as pro-
vided for in said charter : Provided , That alllegal voters re-
siding within the boundary of Elizabethtown ,as establish-
ed by the charterof 1868,shallhave the rightto vote at the
election for trustees under this act. If from any cause
the officeof trustee shall become vacant , the vacancy
shallbe filledby appointment of the presiding judge of
the Hardin county court, which appointees shallhold
theirofficeuntiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified;
and if the office of assessor, treasurer , or collector shall
become vacant ,the vacancy shallbe suppliedby the board
of trusteesfrom time to time : Provided, That on the next
Wednesday following the day of the special electiondi-
rected in thissection,the rightsand powers of all others,
except those elected at that election to discharge any of
the duties of trustees of said town , shall cease .
§ 12. Iffrom any cause the said board of trusteesshall
failto levy and cause to be collectedthe tax authorized
by the provisions of this act,it shallbe the duty of the
judge of the Hardin circuitcourt,upon the petition of
any holder of any bond or bonds issued under this act, to
levy and cause to be collectedthe tax provided for under
thisact, and for thispurpose said circuitjudge shallhave
all the powers conferred on said board of trustees for
levying, assessing, and collectingsaid tax, and may ,for
thepurpose of more effectuallyexecutingitsorders,ap-
point an assessor,treasurer, and collector, and exercise

600 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
any and allpowers necessary to carry into execution its
orders and judgments in reference to the collection of
said tax .
§ 13.All acts and parts of actsinconsistentwith thisact
are hereby repealed .
§14 . This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1024 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe PolytechnicSocietyof Kentucky .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. W. Chenault,J. Lawrence Sinith, Noble
Butler, Thos . E. Jenkins , J. H. Heywood , John Mason
Brown ,A. E Grant, R. M. Kelly, Geo .Ainslie, Dennis
Long ,E. G. Hall, L. P. Yandell,sr.,Benj. F. Avery,W.
B. Belknap ,Geo .W. Morris,Gavin H. Cochran ,and Jobn
Watts Kearny , and their successors,and all others who

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 601
shall hereafter become members of the society hereby
created, be ,and they are hereby ,created a body -corpo-
rate, by the name of the PolytechnicSociety of Ken-
tucky; and by said corporatename are hereby given all
the rights,privileges, and powers of bodies corporate for
the uses and purposes herein set out.
§ 2. The number of members shallnot be limited.
§3 . The objectsof the societyshallbe the establish-
ment of a public and circulating library, and mainte-
nance thereof; the printing and publicationof papers or
works illustrativeof the history of Kentucky , of litera-
ture and science,the encouragement of original, and the
diffusion of knowledge .
§ 4. The corporation may acquire and hold by gift,
purchase , loan, devise, or otherwise, books , pamphlets ,
periodicals,papers,and minerals,to be used as a library
and museum ; and may acquire and hold, in fee or
otherwise, suitable grounds and buildings in which to
keep , preserve, and use its library and museum , and
otherwisecarry out itspurposes; and may make such
rules, regulations and by -laws ,for the acquisition, use,
and enjoyment of its library,museum , and property, for
the controlof itsmembership,and forcarryingout the
objectshereinnamed ,as may be deemed necessary: Pro-
vided, That if the Public Library of Kentucky shallmake
any gift, sale, or transferof propertyto the said Poly-
technic Society,such gift,sale, or transfershallinclude all
moneys ,choses in action, claims,demands ,and rightof
action belonging tosaid Public Library of Kentucky ,either
at law or in equity.
§ 5. The societymay ,in the purchaseor improvement
of real estate and buildings in which to keep , use, and
preserve itslibraryand museum ,etc.,issuebonds ,bearing
interest, not exceeding in amount at one time one hund-
redthousanddollars,which,forthesecurityofthe holder,
shallbe liensof the same degree ,and without preference,
in the nature of a mortgage ,upon the lot and buildings
so to be purchased,erected,or improved : Provided, No
second or subsequent issue of bonds shallbe made until
the firstor prior issue has been deliveredto the society

602 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and canceled;and nothinghereinshallpreventthe socie-
ty from purchasing or improving propertyin any other
manner deemed preferable.
§ 6. The societyshalllease,ifpracticable,such portions
of itsbuilding or buildings as may not be required for its
purpose ; and the money so received for rent shallgo into
the generalfund of the society,and the money received
from any source whatsoever shall go into the general
fund; and the general fund shallbe used- first,to defray
necessary current expenses ; second ,to pay the intereston
any outstanding bonds or other indebtedness The surplus
remaining at the end of any fiscalyear shallbe used to
further the objects of the society and liquidateits out-
standing debts.
§7. The controland disposalof the funds, property,
and estate, and the direction and management of the
concerns of this society,shallbe vested in an executive
committee,to consistof the president,vice president,the
secretaryand ten managers of the society,who shall be
electedannuallyto theirrespectiveofficersby such of
the members of the said societyas are or may be entitled
by the by -laws to vote at such election,or in any other
manner provided in the by -laws .
§ 8. The annual electionof officersshallbe held on the
firstTuesday in April,or on such other days as the socie-
ty may , in and by itsby -laws ,direct.
§ 9. Until the firstannual electionthe present officers
and council shall constitute the executive committee for
said society .
§10 The executive committee shalldefine in by -laws
the duties of the respective officers, and shallrequire of
each officer, whose duty it shallbe to handle or hold or
have charge of any of the property or funds of the socie-
ty,a bond , with sufficientsurety, to secure the faithful
discharge of his duties
§ 11. The executive committee may enact such by -laws
as are deemed necessary to carry out the objectsof the
society in harmony with the charter.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 603
§12. That all the property of this corporation, real,
personal, and mixed , shall be free from all State and
municipal taxation for the period of fiveyears from the
passage of thisact; and in case thischarteror corpora-
tion shalllapsefrom non -user or other cause ,any proper-
ty that may be owned by the corporation shall escheat
to the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and pass under the
supervision of the State Librarian.
§ 13. The Governor of Kentucky ,who may be in office
for the time being , shallbe ex officioa member of the ex-
ecutive committee .
§ 14. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 10 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 1025 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof B. F. Warnock , sheriffof Greenup county.
WHEREAS , William Salliard, David Christian, Samuel
Christian,Calvin Christian,and Wm .Boyd ,were charged
of committing the crime of grand larceny in the county
of Greenup , in the year 1877 , and having made their
escape to the State of Ohio , and were , by the direction
of the authoritiesof Greenup county , arrested by N.
Menshower , the marshal of Ironton, Ohio , and by him
brought to the jail of Greenup county , Kentucky , and
there confined and held for trialfor the crime aforesaid,
and afterwards,to-wit, at the May special term of the
Greenup criminalcourt, 1877, triedand three of them
convicted forsaid crime,and sent to the penitentiary; and
that said court allowed and certified the claim of said
marshal for his fees or services therein , and which were
paid tohim by B.F. Warnock ,sheriffof Greenup county,
amounting to the sum of thirty-three dollars, and there
being no law allowing the Auditor to pay the said claim
out of the Treasury ; and whereas ,one Hiram Baker , a
citizenof Greenup county,Kentucky ,some time in the
year 1877,committed the crime of maliciously shooting
and wounding another with intentto killhim ,and imme-
diatelythereafterfledto the State of Ohio, and was

601 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
arrestedthereby E. P. Merrell,a constable,and brought
to Kentucky and placed in charge of the proper author-
itiesof Greenup county for trial; and the criminal court
of said county,at itsJanuary term, 1878, allowed said
claim, amounting to the sum of twenty -five dollarsand
fiftycents, and which was then paid to him by B. F.
Warnock ,sheriffof Greenup county , and there being no
law by which the Auditoror Treasurercan pay the said
claim ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor draw his warrant on the Treas-
urerforthe sum of fifty-eightdollarsand fiftycents in
favor of B. F. Warnock ,to whom the same shallbe paid
out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

in said county for the years 1876 and 1877; and whereas ,

CHAPTER 1043 .
AN ACT to enable M. M. Teagar,clerkof the Fleming county court, to cor-
rectand perfectcertaincertificatesmade by hisdeputies to deeds.

CHAPTER 1046 .
AN ACT prescribingthe duties of the county court of claims of Bracken
county in relation to turnpike roads in said county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe the duty of the court of claimsfor
Bracken county ,annually to appoint a commissioner to
make annual settlementswith allthe turnpike road com-
paniesin saidcounty in which the county has an interest
or the owner of stock .
§2 . That itshallbe the duty of each turnpike road
company through itstreasurer,on or beforethe firstday
of January , 1879,and annually thereafter,to make a set-
tlement with said commissioner , and filea written state-
ment with him , verifiedby the oath of the treasurer or
presidentof saidcompany , showing the grossreceiptsof
saidcompany ,from whatever source,alsoshowing each
item of expenditureby the company ,and shallalso ex-
hibitthe vouchersupon which such expenditureismade .
The treasurerofeach company shallmake the settlement
as requiredby thisact; and ifrequired,shallexhibitthe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 613
book or books in which the accounts of the company are
kept to said commissioner ; and ifhe shallfailor refuse to
make the settlementas requiredby the provisionsof this
act,shallbe fined not lessthan ten nor more than twenty-
fivedollarsfor each year that he failsso to do within the
time prescribedby thisact,to be recoveredby indictment
by thegrand juryor warrant inthe name oftheCommon-
wealth ,provided the commissioner shall give notice of
thetime and placeformaking such settlement. The fines
assessedunder thisact shallbe paid over to the treasurer
of the county .
§3 Itshallbe the duty of the commissionerto filethe
statement made by the treasurer,and the settlementmade
by him , with the county court clerk of said county, to be
by said clerkproperlyindorsed and filedin his office.
The court of claims shall make a reasonable allowance
to the commissioner for his services .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the county court of claims
also to make an order at the same time directing the
county judge to cast the vote to which the county may
be entitled in the election of officers of the various turn-
pike road companies,eitherby himselfin person,or by
written proxy given to some other person by the county
judge ; and the county judge or his proxy shall cast the
number of votes to which the county is entitledby reason
of the subscriptionto the capital stock of said company ;
the number of votes to be regulated as provided in the
several charters .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1047 .
AN ACT to legalizethe ordersof the Ballard county quarterlycourt.

terlycourt of Ballard county ,and that some of the orders
of said court were not signed by said Ross ; therefore,

Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

ing may be had on the same as if made by the said J. B.

sons,Martinsburg,Elliottcounty,Kentucky . L

shipin saidLodge,be,and they are hereby,incorporated

dens as trustees shall be deemed sufficient notice to said
lodge. That the General Assembly reserves the rightto

CHAPTER 1054 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Martha Hope , of Ohio county .
WHEREAS ,By a verdictof a jury rendered on an inquest
of lunacyheld by the Ohio circuitcourt on the 10th of
November , 1873, Franklin A. Alverson was adjudged a
lunatic,and orderedto be immediatelyconveyed to the
lunaticasylum at Hopkinsville,Kentucky;and itappear-
ing that he could not be received in said asylum tillthe
10th day of December ,A. D. 1876,and that he was taken
careof at greattroubleand expenseby Martha Hope ,and
that said lunatichad no estate,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That theAuditorbe,and ishereby,directedtodraw
a warrant on the Treasury in favorof Martha Hope for
the sum of two hundred and thirty-one dollars and fifty
cents,to be paid out of any money in the Treasury not
otherwise appropriated.
§2 . This act shallbe in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

villeshallhave fullpower and authorityto reduce its
paid-up or cash capitalby purchase of the stock,or by
the surrender of an equal proportion of stock on the part
of all the stockholders of said bank : Provided , That the
cash capital shallnot be reduced below the sum of fifty
thousand dollars .
§2 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 10 , 1878 .

CHAPTER 1056 .
AN ACT for the benefitof J. H. Swift, latesheriffof Calloway county ,
and his sureties .
WHEREAS , J. H. Swift,late sheriffof Calloway ,settled
his revenue for year 1873 without being credited by his
delinquent Auditor'slist; and whereas ,he settledhis en-
tirerevenue for year 1875 without being credited with a
single cent of a delinquent list; and whereas ,judgment
has gone against him and his suretiesfor the total bal-
ance due for the year 1876, without giving any creditfor
delinquent listfor that year ; and whereas ,he has now
filedAuditor'sdelinquent listof 1873,amounting to nine-
ty-one dollarsand sixty-two cents,and a delinquentlist
foryear 1875 of tortydollarsand fifty-seven cents, and a
delinquentlistfor year 1876 of thirty-nine dollarsand
twenty cents; allof which has been duly allowed by the
court of claims of Calloway , and certifiedto the Auditor
as by law required; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby ,instructedto creditsaid suns ,less four per cent.
commission ,upon the judgment against said J. H. Swift
and his sureties for the year 1876 .
§2 . That this act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1060 .
AN ACT to repealan act,entitled"An act to reduce the salaryof the toll-
gate keeper on the Wilderness Turnpike Road ," approved March 13,1872,
and to authorizethe county courtof Laurel county to appoint a commis-
sionerto annual saleof the toll-gate on the Madison Branch of the Wil-
derness Turnpike Road .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled"An act to reduce the sala-
ry of the toll-gate keepers on the Wilderness Turnpike
Road ," approved March 13th, 1872, be , and the same is
hereby ,repealed.
§2 . That the county court of Laurel county is hereby
authorized and empowered to appoint a commissioner
to superintend and control the collectionand disburse-
ment of allfunds arisingfrom the toll-gate on the Madi-
son Branch of the Wilderness Turnpike Road , between
John Pitman's,in Laurel county ,and the terminus of said
road in Madison county .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 633
§3 . That said commissioner ,at the time of his appoint-
ment ,shall execute bond , with good and sufficientsecu-
rity,before the judge of the Laurel county court, to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ,forthe faithfuldischargeof
his duties as such commissioner , and that he willpay out
and disburse any moneys which may come to his hands
as directedby the provisionsof thisact; and said bond
shall be spread of record on the county court records of
Laurel county , and the original shall be filedwith, and
preserved by , the county court clerk of Laurel county ;
and said commissioner shall,immediately after the exe-
cution of such bond , make settlement with the present
commissioners of said road, and shallreceive and receipt
for any money in theirhands belonging to said road , and
shallreportthe amount ,under oath,so receivedby him
to the court of claims of Laurel ,Rockcastle,and Jackson
counties in the year 1878, and shall hold the same to be
used and appropriated by said counties according to their
respectiveinterestsin said road .
§4 . That said commissioner shall,on the firstday of
June , 1878,sellsaid gate for one year to the highest and
best bidder,by public outcry at the court-house door of
Laurel county,notice of which sale shallbe published in
the Mountain Echo , and by at least fivewritten notices
in the vicinityof the gate for thirtydays before the day
of sale,the purchase priceto be paid in threeequal in-
stallmentsduring the year forwhich the gate issold; and
shall take bond from the purchaser, with good security,
payable to himself as commissioner , for each installment
as the same becomes due ; and he shall annually there-
aftermake sale of said gate as herein provided; and the
proceeds arisingfrom the sale of said gate shall be re-
ported by him each year to the county court of claims of
Laurel , Rockcastle, and Jackson counties, and the same
shallbe held and applied by saidcommissioner as ordered
and directed by said courts, under the laws heretofore
governing the divisionof the proceeds arisingfrom said
gate ; said commissioner shall be allowed a reasonable
compensation for his servicesas such,out of the proceeds
arisingfrom the sale of said gate,the same to be allowed

631 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by the county court of claims of Laurel county ,which he
shall retain out of the funds that comes to his hands as
aforesaid .
§ 5. That alllaws in conflictwith this act are hereby
repealed .
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1062 .
AN ACT to regulatethe sale of spirituous, vinous,and malt liquorsin the
county of Greenup .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter the county court, and all other
county and municipal authoritieswithin or for the county
of Greenup ,are prohibited from granting licenseto any
person or persons to vend or disposeof any spirituous,
vinous , or malt liquors, or the mixture of either, in
Greenup county ,in lessquantitiesthan one half gallon;
and alllicenseso granted by any of said authoritiesshall
be absolutelynull and void, and affordno protection
whatever to the person to whom the same isgranted .
§ 2. If any person or persons shallsellor dispose of
any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsinGreenup county
in lessquantitiesthan one half gallon, they shallbe
deemed to have violated the general laws of this Com-
monwealth made and provided againstkeeping a tip-
pling-house,and,upon presentmentand conviction,shall

644 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be subjectedto the penaltiesthereinprovidedforagainst
such offenses .
§3 . Section two of this act shall not be so construed
as to prevent bona fidedruggistsfrom sellingspirituous,
vinous,or malt liquors,in quantities less than one half
gallon,formedicalpurposes,in good faith: Provided,how-
ever, They shallnot so sellsuch liquors,except upon the
written prescriptionof some regular practicingphysician,
in good standing,given in good faith, for medical pur-
poses .
§ 4. Itshallhereafterbe unlawfulforany personorper-
sons to sellspirituous,vinous ,or malt liquorsin Greenup
county in quantitiesover one half gallon and under ten
gallons,to be drank on the premises where sold,or any
other premises other than on the premises belonging to
the purchaser ,or premises in his possessionand under his
control; and ifany partbe sodrank upon premiseswhere
sold,or on premises other than on the premises of the
purchaser , or premises in his possession and under his
control, the vendor thereof shall be guiltyfor each act
of sellingor disposingof, of the offenseof keeping a
tippling-house , and , upon presentment and conviction,
shallbe subjectto allthe penaltiesnow imposed by law
for keeping a tippling-house ,and shall,in addition there-
to,be liable in a civilaction for alldamages resulting
from the use thereofto any person; and any person so
damaged , the cause of which grew out of the use of
such spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors sold or disposed
of contrary to this statute,may sue for and recover the
same ; and if the damage or injury is to a husband ,the
wife may sue,in her own name and in her own right, for
and recover the same ,and may recover for the lossof the
husband'stime,and injuryto hishealth,or injuryto her-
selfor minor children,that may resultfrom such illegal
sale; and if there is no wife,then the minor children of
such vendee shall have the same right to sue for and
recoverthe same as ifgiven to the wife,suing by their
next friend,for their use; and if neither wife or minor
children sue for the same within three months ,then the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 645
rightof action shallvest in the Commonwealth ,who may
sue for and recover the same , and the amount so recov-
ered shall go , one half to the informant or prosecutor,
and the other one half go to the benefitof the common
school in said county ,to be distributedto them pro rata,
according to the number of scholars in each school dis-
trictin the county .
§ 5. If any owner of real estatewithin the county of
Greenup shallhereafteruse,or knowingly sufferthe same
to be used by others,forthe purpose of vending and other-
wise disposing of spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin or
upon the house and premises in and on which the same
is kept for the purposes of sale or disposition, or is sold
and disposed of contrary to the provisionsof this,or con-
trary to the provisions of the general laws of thisCom-
monwealth ,shallbe held liablefor the satisfactionof any
judgment that may be recovered against the vendor of
such liquorsfor the illegalsale or dispositionof the same ,
eitherfor the recovery had against him ,in the name of
the Commonwealth ,on any person suing for the damage
givenby thisact; and saidhouse orpremisesshallnot be
exempted under the exemption laws of thisStateforany
recoveries under this act, or under the general laws of
thisState,but the whole premises shall be held subject,
and soldin satisfactionof, such judgments .
§6 . If any physicianshallhereaftergiveto any person
or persons a prescriptionfor spirituous,vinous,or malt
liquors,forany purposes otherthan medicalpurposes,in
good faith,or should any officersnamed in thisact do
any act which they are prohibitedfrom doing ,or failor
refuseto perform any duty requiredof them by thisact,
they, or eitherof them ,so offending or neglecting or re-
fusing to perform their duties, shall, upon presentment
and conviction,be fined in any sum not lessthan twenty-
fivenor more than one hundred dollars,or imprisonment
not less than ten nor more than thirtydays ,or both so
fined and imprisoned, in the discretionof the jury or
court trying the case.

646 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§7 . When thisact shalltake effect, alllocal laws now
in forcein relationto the sellingand vending intoxicating
beveragesinGreenup county,and made applicablealone
to said county , shall stand repealed,but thereby not re-
pealing any of the generallaws of thisState in said
county .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1063 .
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled " An act to amend and revise the charter
of Millersburg,Bourbon county," approved February 23 , 1874.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the town of Millersburg , in
Bourbon county,approved February 23, 1874, is hereby
so amended that the policejudge of said town shallhere-
afterhave jurisdictionin civilcases and proceedings con-
current with the Bourbon quarterly and circuitcourts,
where the amount in controversy, exclusive of interest
and costs,exceedsfiftydollars,and is under one hundred
dollars .
§2 . That the said police court shallbe a court of rec-
ord, and thejudge thereof shallact as clerk of the same ;
and certifiedcopies of the records of said court,over the
signature of the judge , shallbe evidence in any court of
this Commonwealth . That the pleadings in said court
shallbe oral,and regulatedby the CivilCode . That before
a summons shallbe issued on a claim before said judge
exceeding fiftydollars,exclusive of interestand costs,a
tax of fiftycentsshallbe paid to saidjudge,to be taxed
as costs, and said judge shallreport and account for such
tax as required by law in the collectionof fines due the
Commonwealth .
§ 3. That said police court shallhave exclusive origi-
nal jurisdictionof all violationsof the ordinances and
by -laws of saidtown ,and of allcriminaland penal cases

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 647
under the general laws ,when the misdemeanor or crime
iscommitted in said town . Said court shallhave no ap-
pellatejurisdiction; and alljudgments of said court, in
criminaland penal cases,under the generallaws,where
the fine imposed does not exceed fifteendollars,or the
imprisonment ten days , or for any violation of a town
ordinance ,or in civil cases where the matter in contro-
versy, exclusive of interest and costs, does not exceed
twenty dollars, shall be final and conclusive ; but in all
othercasesan appeal may be taken,under theprovisions
of the Civiland Criminal Codes of Practice,within sixty
days afterthe renditionof the judgment ; which appeal
shallbe taken directlyto the Bourbon circuitcourt. The
marshal shallexecute process from said court, unless
otherwise directedby the party or his attorney in whose
behalf the same isprocured , which directionmay be ver-
bal. Said judge shallhave power ,however ,to directhis
process to be executed by any constable or the sheriffof
Bourbon county , as well as by the marshal of said town .
§ 4. That the persons entitledto vote for trustees of
said town shall, at the time and place of the election
thereof, and every two years thereafter,elect a judicial
officer, to be styled the policejudge of Millersburg,who
shallhold his officefor the term of two years,or untilhis
successor is elected and qualified; and whenever a va-
cancy occurs in said officeby death or otherwise,it shall
be filledby an electionforthe remainder of the term ,upon
due notice of the time and place of election.
§ 5. The said policejudge shallhave jurisdictionin said
town , and the town precinct, of allcivilcauses of which
justicesof the peace have or may hereafterhave jurisdic-
tion; in allcriminal cases the said policejudge shallhave
the jurisdictionas a courtof inquiry now or hereaftergiven
by law to two justices of the peace of said county , and
shallproceed in likemanner as saidjusticesare required
by law in criminal cases. The police judge shall have
jurisdictionof all cases of motions and suits against the
treasurer, marshal , clerk, or other officersof said town ,
for allsums of money , or other thing,which may be due
from them ,or eitherof them . He shallhave fullpower

648 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and authority to grant injunctions, attachments ,restrain-
ing orders,writs of ne exeatand habeascorpus,and to issue
warrants in civilor criminal cases,under the same rules
and regulations prescribedby the several acts authoriz-
ing justicesof the peace or the county judge to grant
injunctions,attachments,restrainingorders,writs of ne
exeat and habeas corpus, and to issue warrants . He shall
have power to issue summons for witnesses to give evidence
in causes pending before him , or to give theirdepositions,
and upon theirfailureto attend,to issuecompulsory pro-
cess to cause theirattendance . He shallhave power to
fineand imprison for contempt : Provided, The fine shall
not exceed fivedollarsand the imprisonment eight hours .
He shallhave power to take depositions, and certifythe
same as justicesof the peace now have ; and his certifi-
cate to such depositions shall have the same effectas
though made by a justiceof the peace. He shallhave a
jury to try all matters before him , if desired by either
party, ifthe amount in controversy ismore than sixteen
dollars. The policejudge shallbe entitledto the follow-
ing fees,to-wit : for issuing a peace or other warrant ,
fiftycents; for swearing a jury and presidingover itin
any case,exceptbreachesof the peace or misdemeanors,
to be taxed against the defendant ,iffound guilty,and for
swearing a jury and superintending a trialof a writ of
forcibleentryand detainer,two dollars; forpresidingat
an examining court,to be paid out of the Treasury , two
dollarsper day ; fortaking recognizance to keep the peace ,
one dollar,to be paid by the applicant,and taxed as costs
against the defendant ; for taking recognizance in court,
thirtycents; for taking depositions, same fees allowed
examiners ; for granting an injunction, attachment , or
restrainingorder,or writof ne ereat,each fiftycents; for
each order of injunction, fiftycents; foreach copy there-
of, twenty-five cents; for each order of attachiment,fifty
cents; foreach copy thereof,twenty-fivecents; for each
summons in ordinaryor equity,fittycents; foreach copy
thereof,twenty-fivecents;foreach orderofdelivery,fifty
cents; foreach copy thereof,twenty-fivecents; foreach
subpœna , twenty -five cents; for each subpæna duces te

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 649
cum , fiftycents; for each distresswarrant for rent, fifty
cents; for each execution, including indorsements and
returnthereof,to be charged when issued, sixtycents;
for a copy of any execution, thirtycents; for issuinga
summons for a garnishee,fiftycents; for each warrant
of arrest for witness or witnesses , twenty -five cents ; for
docketing a cause at each term ,ten cents; for rendering
a judgment,twenty-fivecents; for recordingan original
judgment without a jury,twenty -fivecents; foreach copy
ofa judgment ,twenty-fivecents;foreach orderofcourtin
any action,twenty-fivecents;foreach copy thereof,twen-
ty cents; for taxing costs for each party or parties, at
each term when judgment for costs is rendered , or on
finaljudgment twenty cents; for a copy of each order of
arrestin ordinaryor equity,twenty-fivecents; forenter-
ing and giving a post note of an estray, affidavit, and
certifying, & c., fiftycents; for an order of commitment
in any case,fiftycents; forallservicesforwhich feesare
not speciallyallowed herein, the policejudge shallbe
allowed to charge and receive the same now allowed by
law; and allother feesof saidjudge shallbe the same
as allowed by law to justicesof the peace forlikeser-
vices .
§ 6. Any of the officersaforesaidwho shall fail, neg-
lect, or refuse to execute any warrant ,summons ,or pro-
cess,and make due return of the same , shallbe finednot
lessthan twenty dollarsupon motion in said court of any
party aggrieved,ten days' notice having been given to
saidofficer. Any officerwho shallfailto collectany writ
offierifacias,or executeany capiaspro fine issuedfrom
the police court,and make due return thereof according
to law ,shall, with his securities,be subject to allthe dam-
ages and penaltiesnow imposed by law upon sheriffsfor
failingto collect,return,and pay over money when col-
lectedon writsof fierifaciasand capiasprofine.
،،
§7 . A return of " not found " on a capias pro fine,or of
no propertyfound," inwhole orin part,on afierifacias,
issuedon any judgment in saidpolicecourt,shallauthor-
ize an attachment out of chancery in favor of the Com-
monwealth ,or board of trusteesof Millersburg, or other

650 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
plaintiff, against the choses in action and other effectsof
the defendant or defendants , in the same manner that
the returnof " no property" authorizesan attachmentin
chancery on judgments rendered in the circuitcourts.
§ 8. That allpersonsadjudged to pay finesin the Mil-
lersburgpolicecourt,who do not pay or replevythe same ,
shall be forthwith confined in the station-house of said
town until the fines and costs of prosecution assessed
against them are discharged, at the rate of one dollar
per day for each day's confinement ; but the police judge
may requireand have power to compel any able-bodied
persons confined in said station-house , for a failureto
pay or replevyfinesimposed by saidcourt,or on a judg-
ment of imprisonment by said court, to work out said
fines and costs under the supervision of the marshal or
otherperson appointedby the court,on any ofthe streets,
alleys, or publicgrounds of said town , and allow such
personsone dollarper day for each day'swork as afore-
said; or, in case of a judgment of imprisonment, allow
the prisoner,for each day's work , a creditof one day on
the judgment . In all cases where the defendant works
out the costs adjudged against him , the officersto whom
said costs are due shall be allowed the same out of the
town treasury .
§9 . Whenever ,from any cause,the judge of said court
failsto attend the court,or,ifin attendance ,cannot prop-
erlypreside in any cause or causes pending in said court,
any justiceof the peace forBourbon countymay preside
in his place; and the person so presidingshall,during the
period he acts,have all the powers and be liable to all
the responsibilitiesof the police judge , and shall receive
allfees accruing during said period.
§ 10. That the terms of said police court shall be fixed
by the judge of saidcourt,and held monthly. Noticeof
the time and place of holding said courts,or any altera-
tion thereof, shallbe advertisedby printed advertise-
ments posted in fiveor more public places in said precinct
fifteen(15) days before the beginning of any term so
fixedor alteredby saidjudge.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 651
§ 11. That the finesrecoveredin saidpolicecourtin
the name of,or in favorof,the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky,or in the name of the town of Millersburg, and all
forfeituresincurredby reason of the breach of any bail
bond taken or entered intoin saidpolicecourt,arehere-
by granted to said town .
§ 12. That the board of trustees of Millersburg shall
have the rightto appointa collectorof the taxesof said
town ,in place of the marshal . The town collectorshall,
from and after the day he receives the tax-book in each
year, proceed to collectthe taxes due said town ; and
upon failure of the persons bound therefor to pay the
same ,may distrainthe personalpropertyowned by or in
therightfulpossessionof the person from whom the tax
is due ,notwithstanding the existence of any lien upon
the same ; and may proceed to sellthetitleof such per-
son in so much thereof as will pay the taxes due from
him , and costs and expenses of sale. The sale must be
made for cash, and be made on the public square in said
town . The time and terms of sale shall be advertised
by writtenor printednoticesposted up in said town ten
days previousto theday of sale. Ifthe collectormake
illegal or unreasonable seizure and distress for taxes ,he
shallbe liablein damages to the party aggrieved .
§ 13. On alltaxes due to said town not paid to the col-
lector by the firstday of September in each year ,there
shall be added ,as penalty, ten per cent.on the amount
due,to be collectedand accounted forby the collector
as other taxes . Such damages are for the benefit of the
town , and not the marshal or collector.
§ 14. If there be no personalpropertywhich the col-
lector can distrain for tax due on real estate within said
town , or if the personal property be insufficientto pay
the whole of said tax , and the owner or owners of such
real estate shall fail to pay said tax on the 31st day
of December of the year in which the same is due ,the
collectorshall,on the firstday of some county court held
in and for Bourbon county , at the court-house door in
the cityof Paris, and , from day to day ,until completed ,

652 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
proceed to sellsuch real estate,or so much thereof as
may be necessaryto pay the tax thereon,togetherwith
the ten per cent.penaltyin the next preceding section
specified, and the costs and expenses of sale, at public
auction,to the highest bidder,for cash . The terms ,time,
and place of sale, together with a description of such
real estate, and the name of the owner or owners , or
occupants thereof, and the amount of tax and penalty
due thereon, shallbe advertisedby printed noticesposted
up at the court-house door in the cityof Paris,and five
otherpublicplacesin Bourbon county,twenty days pre-
vious to the day of sale.
$ 15. The collectorshall,so soon as practicable after
making saleof any propertyfortaxes,make a fullreport
of his proceedings to the board of trustees,including in
saidreporta listof the propertysold,and the names of
the owners or occupantsof said property,names of the
purchasers,amounts forwhich the propertywas sold,and
a descriptionof said property; and he shall also hand in
with his report a copy of the advertisements, together
with an affidavit of some disinterested citizen of Bourbon
county ,statingthat said advertisement was posted up as
requiredby thisact,which affidavitshallform a part of
the collector'sreport.
§ 16. All real estatesoldby the collectorfor taxes may
be redeemed by the owner ,or his representative,at any
time within two years from the day of sale,by paying to
thepurchaser,or hisrepresentative,the purchase money ,
and twenty per cent.per annum from the day of sale until
redeemed . The person thus redeeming shalltake the re-
ceiptof the purchaser,or his representative, for the re-
demption money , and lodge the same with the clerkof
the said board of trustees,to be filedwith the collector's
reportof sale. Ifthe purchaser,or hisrepresentativeor
assignee,does not residein Bourbon county , or cannot be
found therein,the owner of the property,or his represent-
ative,may make affidavitof thefact,and pay the amount
of the purchase money ,with the interestaforesaid, to the
town collector,which affidavitand receiptshallbe lodged
with the clerk aforesaid,to be filedwith the collector's

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 653
reportof sale: Provided,Infantsshallhave one year after
arrivingat fullage to redeem their real estate, upon the
same terms and in the same manner as herein provided
forpersons of fullage .
§ 17. The town collectorwho sellsreal estatefor taxes,
or his successorin office,must convey by deeds the title
soldto the purchaser,or hisassignee,or his heirsor dev-
sees,ifthe same isnot redeemed as providedin thisact;
and said collectormaking the sale may make said deed
after his term of officehas expired. The deed shallbe
acknowledged before the clerk of the Bourbon county
court,and be recorded in his office, and shallpass to the
purchaser a perfecttitle.
§ 18. The compensation of said collectorshallnot ex-
ceed fiveper cent.of the gross amount collectedby him ,
and costs now allowed by law to the marshal for similar
services. The collector,when appointed in place of the
marshal ,if one is so appointed by the said board of trus-
tees,shallgive bond ,with security, payable to the board
of trustees,which bond shallbe approved by said board
before he shallenter upon his duties; said collectorshall
settlewith the treasurerof the town every thirtydays ,and
pay over in cash the amount collected,and take his re-
ceipt for all amounts paid over to him , showing how
much is paid. Any failureto so settlefor more than five
days afterthe firstday of each month , shallforfeitall
compensation due him from said town , and shallvacate
his office. The policejudge of Millersburgis hereby
given jurisdictionto try said collectorout of term time ,
on warrant issuedby him ; and if,on said trial,itshall
appear that said collectorisguiltyof a violationof this
act,itshallbe the duty of said judge to enter a judgment
as above directed herein . The board of trustees shall
have the right at any time to demand the tax-books of
the marshal ,by giving him ten days' notice of such de-
mand , and may appoint a collectoras provided in his
stead .

with the treasurerthereof every three months ,to-wit : on

654 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the firstday of May ,August,November ,and February,
and any failureby them to settleand pay over to said
treasurerany amount in theirhands for more than five
days afterthe firstday of each said months,shallsub-
ject the party so failingto the same penalty herein de-
clared against the collectorof tax in the preceding section.
When said marshal or clerksettlewith said treasurer,they
shall take his receipt showing the amount paid him in
money .
§20 . That sectioneleven (11) of " An act to amend and
revise the charter of Millersburg, Bourbon county ," ap-
proved February 23 , 1874,be so amended that a written
notice of the adoption of the ordinance requiringthe pav-
ing and repairing of sidewalks,signed by the chairman
or clerk of the board of trustees , shall be served on the
owner or occupant of the lotor fractionof lot in front of
which such sidewalk is required to be paved or repaired,
by the marshal or such officeras the board may designate.
Said officershall serve said notice by delivering a true
copy thereofto such owner or occupant, and he shall
return the original,properly indorsed as to the manner in
which he executed it,to the clerk of said board of trus-
tees,who shall enter the same upon the record-book of
said board , and such record shallbe prima facieevidence
that said notice was given ; and an attestedcopy of said
record may be used in the trialof any cause ,and shall
have the same force as copies of records attestedby a
county court clerk now authorizedby law . The costs
and charges incurred by the board of trustees under said
sectioneleven shallbe paid by the owners of the lotor
fraction of lot in front of which the sidewalk has been
paved or repaired; and said costsand charges may be re-
covered in the name of the board of trustees of the town
of Millersburg,in the police court of Millersburg, if the
sum of said costsand charges does not exceed one hund-
red dollars,and if over that sum ,by suitin the Bourbon
circuit court in the name of said board ; and said board of
trusteesshallhave a lienupon the lotor lots,part of lot
or lots,in frontof which said board has caused the side-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 655
walk or sidewalks to be paved or repaired forthe amount
of costs and charges thus incurred .
§21 . The regular meetings of said board of trustees
shallbe held semi -monthly ; and they shallreceive a com-
pensation of fiftycents for each regularmeeting they
may attend : Provided, Said board of trustees may hold
specialmeetings if, in theirjudgment ,the same should be
necessary; but they shall receive no compensation for
theirattendance upon said specialmeetings .
§22 . The board of trustees of said town shall have
power to contractdebts,by borrowing money or other-
wise , to improve the streetsand alleys of said town ,
and to erect other public improvements for the benefit
and advantages of said town ,and to give bonds for the
same ,never,however ,to bear a greater interestthan ten
per cent.,in order that provisionmay be made by said
trusteesfor the payment of the intereston allsuch debts
as may be contracted, and for creating a sinking fund by
which the principalof such debts shallbe paid: Pro-
vided,however, That the principalof the debt or debts so
incurred by said trustees shall, under no circumstances,
exceed one thousand dollars, and that said trustees shall
have no power to create any debt,by borrowing money ,
exceeding one hundred dollars,without the approval of the
majorityofthe votersentitledtovotefortrusteeswho may
vote on the question of borrowing money ; and to ascertain
the willof the voters, a pollmay be opened in the same
way as forthe electionof trustees,and under likerulesand
regulations,and not to be closed sooner than fiveo'clock
in the afternoon ; and when a majority of those who vote
shallbe in favorof borrowing money for such improve-
ments,the trusteesmay proceed to do so: Provided,how-
ever, The sum to be borrowed shallbe stated before the
pollis opened; and the trusteesshallhave no authority
toborrow any largersum . The bonds may be issuedfor
the payment of said debts,shallbe signedby the chair-
man ,and countersigned by the clerk.
§23 . Nothing in this act shallbe construed to abridge
or circumscribe any power or authoritynow granted by

656 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
law to thetrusteesor policejudge of the town of Millers-
burg .
§ 24. All acts and parts of acts heretofore passed , in-
consistentwith the provisionsof thisact,are repealed,
and all such acts and parts of acts in harmony with this
are continued in force aad re -enacted .
§25 . That the corporatelimitsof the town of Millers-
burg , Bourbon county ,be , and are hereby , extended in

nerswith the grounds oftheKentucky Wesleyan College;

Wesleyan Collegeto the M. and L Turnpike; then diag-
onallyacrosssaidpiketothe boundary lineofS. B. Dod-
son and Mrs. Nunn ; then with said boundary line in a

road in a southerlydirectionto where said railroadtouches
the present town limits; thence with said boundary to
oppositebank of Hinkstoncreek; then alongthe bank of
said creek in a westerly and northerlydirectionto a point
opposite the western boundary of the town ; thence along
said boundary in a north northeastdirectionto the begin-
ning .
§26 . This actshalltake effectinitspassage.
Approved April 10, 1878.

tian College."

of Kentucky :

ors and of the facultyof said college shall be members
of the ChristianChurch ; that each member of the faculty,
at the time of taking his seat as a teacher in the said col-
lege,shallbe a member of the congregation in Columbia ,
and that not less than one third of the board of directors
shall hold membership with the said congregation ; also,
that the eldersof the saidcongregationshallbe ex officio
members of the said board of directors .
" SEC .5. That any member of the said ChristianChurch ,
who shall give to the endowment fund of the institution
the sum of fivehundred dollarsor more ,shall be entitled
to one vote in the management of the finances of the
said college for each five hundred dollars that he shall so
bestow ; but such donor shall not be entitled to hold office
in the board of directorsunless he shallfirstbe regularly
elected as a member thereof ."

power to create such offices, elect such officers, and to
make such rulesfor theirown government ,as shallbe by
them deemed necessary to carry out the objects of the
corporation; that they shall have power to allow or hin-
der the organization of any literarysocietiesamong the
students of said collegethat they may deem proper ; and
that such literarysocietiesas are organized with their
consent,under theirsupervision,shall,in theircorporate
capacity, be entitledto allthe rightsand privilegesof
any literarysocietiesin thisCommonwealth .

passage .
Approved April 10 , 1878.

dollarsand twenty cents($53 20),beingthe amount due
him for a delinquent listallowed him by the county court

CHAPTER 1082 .
AN ACT to aid school districtNo. 13 , Bourbon county .
WHEREAS , In consequence of building a school-house in
districtNo. 13, Bourbon county, no school was taught
therein,and the money apportioned the districthas been
returned to the Treasury ,and has not yet been bonded to
the creditof the county ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sum of seventy-sixdollarsand fortycents,
being the amount apportioneddistrictNo. 13,Bourbon
county,forthe year ending June 30th, 1877,be, and is
hereby,appropriatedtosaiddistrict,tobe paidtheteacher
of the school of said district; and the Superintendent of
Public Instructionis hereby authorized to draw his draft
on the Auditor in favor of the same , when the commis-
sionershallreportthat a school has been taught for five
months during the school year ending June 30th, 1878.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

CHAPTER 1083 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John Cravens,a pauper idiot.
WHEREAS , No inquest was held in the case of John
Cravens, a pauper idiotof Marion county, for thirteen
months and nineteen(19) days previoustoFebruary 8th,
1878; and it appearing to this General Assembly that
saidJohn Cravens,a pauper idiot,isstillaliveand in the
care of his committee ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasurer in
favor of F. K. Beaven , committee of John Cravens , a
pauper idiot,foreighty-fourdollarsand thirty-seven cents
($ 84 37).
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
CHAPTER 1084 .
AN ACT forthe preservationand production of quails and partridgesin
Mason county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . It shall be unlawful for any person willfullyto
catch,kill,or in any manner destroy,or pursue with such
intent, within the boundaries of Mason county, during
the years 1878 and 1879,and untilthe 20th day of Octo-
ber, 1880,any quailor partridge. The possessionby any
person of any such quailor partridgein the boundaries
of said county,withinthe saidtime,shallbe considered
prima facieevidenceof a violationof the provisionsof
thissection. Any person violatingthe provisionsof this
section shall be fined not less than five nor more than
twenty dollars.
§2 : The penalty fixed in this act shall be recoverable
upon proper proceedings before a justiceof the peace or
magistrate in Mason county .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 671
§3. All finesaccruingunder thisact shallbe equally
divided between the county attorney,whose duty it shall
be to prosecute all persons violating the provisions of
thisact,and the school fund of the county .
§4 . This act shalltake effectsixtydays from and after
itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878 .
CHAPTER 1085 .

of Louisville," approved April 19th, 1873 .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. No person shallbe eligibleas trusteeof said
schools who has not attained the age of thirtyyears,
and is not the owner of real estate or a housekeeper ,
who is not a citizenof the United States,and a bona fide
residentof the ward forwhich he may be chosen,or who
has not been a resident of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky for five years, and of the cityof Louisvillethree
years next precedinghis election; or who holds or dis-
charges any officeto which he has been,under existing
laws,electedby popular vote; or who is,at the time of
his election, interestedin any contractwith the said
board of trusteesof public schools; or who holds any
officeof trust or salary in any corporation which holds
any contract with said board of trusteesor the city of
Louisville, the terms,rates,or prices whereof are subject
tomodificationorenforcementby saidboardof trustees
or cityof Louisville; or whose father,son,brother,wife,
daughter,or sisteris employed as a teacheror professor
in theMale High School,Female High School,or any of
the publicschoolsof Louisville,at the time of hiselec-
tion; or who is a member of either board of council,or
interestedin the purchase or sale of books or stationery,
or both, or agent therefor. And if, after election, any
member of said board of trustees should remove out of
the ward for which he was chosen ,or became a candidate
foror accept any officewhich would renderhim ineli-

672 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
gible,or should become interestedin any contractwith
said board of trustees; or if his father,son,brother, wife,
daughter ,or sister, should be employed as a teacher or
professorin eitherof the said high schools or other public
schools of Louisville, his seat shall thereby be vacated ,
and thevacancy suppliedas hereinbeforeprovided.
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1878.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 675
CHAPTER 1089 ..
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled " An act to establish and maintain a
graded freeschoolin Harrodsburg,Kentucky ."
WHEREAS , The question of taxation for the graded free
schoolin Harrodsburg,Kentucky,providedforin the fifth
section of the act,entitled " An act to establish and main-
tain a graded free school in the town of Harrodsburg ,
Kentucky ," approved March 15th,1876,was not submitted
to the qualifiedwhite voters of said town as prescribed
in said section ; and whereas , the friends of said graded
school have , by private subscription, purchased a large
and commodious building for said graded school,by rea-
son of which there is no necessityfor funds to be raised
by taxation for the erectionof buildings for said graded
school ; and whereas , the friends of said graded school
desire to submit to the qualifiedvoters of said town the
question as to whether they willhereafterrun said graded
schoolby taxation; and not having the power to testsaid
questionunder the originalact,forremedy whereof,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That upon the applicationof the board of trustees
of said graded school,through itschairman ,itshallbe the
duty of the board of trusteesof the town of Harrodsburg
to submit to the qualified white voters of said town the
questionas to whether they willempower the saidschool
trusteesto annuallylay and collecta tax on the property
of the white persons owning property in said town sub-
ject to taxation for State revenue purposes,not to exceed
thirtycentson the one hundred dollarsin any one year,
to be used in common school education in said town , as
provided forin said act to which thisis an amendment ,
except that none of said tax isto be used for the erection
of school buildings: Provided, That printed notices of
the time and place of the taking of said vote shall be
posted at leasttwo weeks before taking of said vote,and
alsoby two weekly publicationsin any paper printedat
Harrodsburg ,Kentucky : And providedfurther, That if the
questionof taxationshould be defeatedat any time,the

676 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
same shallnot be taken as exhausting the power to take
as many more votes on the question as may be deemed
proper ,until said tax shallhave been passed ; but in the
taking of any subsequent vote afterthe first, the same
notice shallbe given as hereinbefore required. The vote
shall be taken and returned and declared in the manner
as set forth in the fifth section of said original act; and
if said tax shall be voted by a majority of the qualified
votes cast,itshallbe laid,collected,paid over,and expend-
ed as provided in said original act to which this is an
amendment .
§2 . This act shallbe in forcefrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 10 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 1090 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof William F. Ward , of Muhlenburg county.
WHEREAS , On the 9th of October , 1865, James Miller
and Mary M. Miller, of Warrick county ,in the State of
Indiana, made and acknowledged their warranty deed to
certain real estatein Muhlenburg county ,Kentucky ,by
which they conveyed all theirinterestin the real estate
thereinmentioned to William F. Ward , of said Muhlen-
burg county , and on the 23d of October , 1865,George W.
Kinder , Elizabeth D. Kinder , his wife, of said Warrick
county ,Indiana ,made and acknowledged theirwarranty
deed for one seventh part of the same land to the said
William F. Ward , which deeds were duly authenticated
in the State of Indiana , but were not returned to the said
William F. Ward inthe time requiredby the statutesof
this Commonwealth for record; forthis cause the county
court clerk of said Muhlenburg county refusesto receive
and record same ; for remedy whereof ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court clerk of Muhlenburg county
be , and he is hereby , authorized and directed to receive
said deeds, and put same to record in his office, upon
thepayment of the tax and feesallowedby law,and said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 677
recording shall have the same effect,as against the grant-
ors in said deeds, as if they had been recorded within the
time fixed by statute.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage ..
Approved April 10,1878 .

county .

of Kentucky :

hereby ,directedto draw his warrant on the Treasurer in
favor of James R. Dean , school commissioner of Law-
rence county , forthe sum of thirtydollars and seventy-
two cents($30 72),thatbeingthe amount paid by James
R. Dean ,school commissioner of Lawrence county , out
of his own funds,to school districtNo. 44 ,as shown in
his settlement,on account of sixteenpupilswhich had
been reported to the said James R. Dean ,school commis-
sioner,by the trustee, and ,by mistake , was not placed by
him inhisreportforsaidschoolyear,and therebycausing
a deficitof thirtydollarsand seventy-two cents ($30 72).
§2 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 10 , 1878 .
CHAPTER 1093 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John A. Webb ,of Russellcounty.
WHEREAS , A mistake having been made in districtNo.
37 ,Russell county ,in reporting the number of children,

that would have been drawn on twenty children if re-
ported,being thirty-two dollars and twenty cents; now ,
therefore ,

678 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

of Kentucky :

to draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of John A.
Webb ,forthesum ofthirty-two dollarsand twenty cents,
to be paid out of the unbonded surplus belonging to the
school fund of said county .
§2 . That this act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 10, 1878.
